** (5.1).

** (5.1).

# Enumeration: CrosshairMode | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the crosshair mode.

Enumeration Members[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Normal[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#normal "Direct link to Normal")

> **Normal**: `0`

This mode allows crosshair to move freely on the chart.

* * *

### Magnet[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#magnet "Direct link to Magnet")

> **Magnet**: `1`

This mode sticks crosshair's horizontal line to the price value of a single-value series or to the close price of OHLC-based series.

* * *

### Hidden[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#hidden "Direct link to Hidden")

> **Hidden**: `2`

This mode disables rendering of the crosshair.

* * *

### MagnetOHLC[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#magnetohlc "Direct link to MagnetOHLC")

> **MagnetOHLC**: `3`

This mode sticks crosshair's horizontal line to the price value of a single-value series or to the open/high/low/close price of OHLC-based series.

*   [Enumeration Members](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#enumeration-members)
    *   [Normal](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#normal)
        
    *   [Magnet](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#magnet)
        
    *   [Hidden](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#hidden)
        
    *   [MagnetOHLC](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode#magnetohlc)

---

# Enumeration: LastPriceAnimationMode | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the type of the last price animation for series such as area or line.

Enumeration Members[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Disabled[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#disabled "Direct link to Disabled")

> **Disabled**: `0`

Animation is always disabled

* * *

### Continuous[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#continuous "Direct link to Continuous")

> **Continuous**: `1`

Animation is always enabled.

* * *

### OnDataUpdate[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#ondataupdate "Direct link to OnDataUpdate")

> **OnDataUpdate**: `2`

Animation is active after new data.

*   [Enumeration Members](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#enumeration-members)
    *   [Disabled](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#disabled)
        
    *   [Continuous](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#continuous)
        
    *   [OnDataUpdate](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode#ondataupdate)

---

A solid line.

### Dotted[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle#dotted "Direct link to Dotted")

A dotted line.

* * *

# Enumeration: PriceScaleMode | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the price scale mode.

Enumeration Members[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Normal[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#normal "Direct link to Normal")

> **Normal**: `0`

Price scale shows prices. Price range changes linearly.

* * *

### Logarithmic[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#logarithmic "Direct link to Logarithmic")

> **Logarithmic**: `1`

Price scale shows prices. Price range changes logarithmically.

* * *

### Percentage[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#percentage "Direct link to Percentage")

> **Percentage**: `2`

Price scale shows percentage values according the first visible value of the price scale. The first visible value is 0% in this mode.

* * *

### IndexedTo100[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#indexedto100 "Direct link to IndexedTo100")

> **IndexedTo100**: `3`

The same as percentage mode, but the first value is moved to 100.

*   [Enumeration Members](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#enumeration-members)
    *   [Normal](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#normal)
        
    *   [Logarithmic](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#logarithmic)
        
    *   [Percentage](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#percentage)
        
    *   [IndexedTo100](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode#indexedto100)

---

# Enumeration: TickMarkType | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the type of a tick mark on the time axis.

Enumeration Members[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Year[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#year "Direct link to Year")

> **Year**: `0`

The start of the year (e.g. it's the first tick mark in a year).

* * *

### Month[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#month "Direct link to Month")

> **Month**: `1`

The start of the month (e.g. it's the first tick mark in a month).

* * *

### DayOfMonth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#dayofmonth "Direct link to DayOfMonth")

> **DayOfMonth**: `2`

A day of the month.

* * *

### Time[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#time "Direct link to Time")

> **Time**: `3`

A time without seconds.

* * *

### TimeWithSeconds[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#timewithseconds "Direct link to TimeWithSeconds")

> **TimeWithSeconds**: `4`

A time with seconds.

*   [Enumeration Members](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#enumeration-members)
    *   [Year](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#year)
        
    *   [Month](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#month)
        
    *   [DayOfMonth](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#dayofmonth)
        
    *   [Time](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#time)
        
    *   [TimeWithSeconds](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType#timewithseconds)

# Enumeration: TrackingModeExitMode | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Determine how to exit the tracking mode.

By default, mobile users will long press to deactivate the scroll and have the ability to check values and dates. Another press is required to activate the scroll, be able to move left/right, zoom, etc.

Enumeration Members[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### OnTouchEnd[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#ontouchend "Direct link to OnTouchEnd")

> **OnTouchEnd**: `0`

Tracking Mode will be deactivated on touch end event.

* * *

### OnNextTap[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#onnexttap "Direct link to OnNextTap")

> **OnNextTap**: `1`

Tracking Mode will be deactivated on the next tap event.

*   [Enumeration Members](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#enumeration-members)
    *   [OnTouchEnd](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#ontouchend)
        
    *   [OnNextTap](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode#onnexttap)

---

Version: 5.1

On this page

Search the nearest left item

* * *

### None[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MismatchDirection#none "Direct link to None")

> **None**: `0`

Do not search

* * *

### NearestRight[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MismatchDirection#nearestright "Direct link to NearestRight")

On this page

Enumeration representing the sign of a marker.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Negative[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MarkerSign#negative "Direct link to Negative")

> **Negative**: `-1`

Represents a negative change (-1)

* * *

### Neutral[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MarkerSign#neutral "Direct link to Neutral")

> **Neutral**: `0`

Represents no change (0)

* * *

### Positive[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MarkerSign#positive "Direct link to Positive")

> **Positive**: `1`

Represents a positive change (1)

# Function: createChart() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **createChart**(`container`, `options`?): [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi)

This function is the simplified main entry point of the Lightweight Charting Library with time points for the horizontal scale.

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **container**: `string` | `HTMLElement`

ID of HTML element or element itself

• **options?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`TimeChartOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions)
\>

Any subset of options to be applied at start.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart#returns "Direct link to Returns")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi)

An interface to the created chart

*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart#returns)

# Function: isUTCTimestamp() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/isUTCTimestamp#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **isUTCTimestamp**(`time`): `time is UTCTimestamp`

Check if a time value is a UTC timestamp number.

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/isUTCTimestamp#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **time**: [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

The time to check.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/isUTCTimestamp#returns "Direct link to Returns")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`time is UTCTimestamp`

`true` if `time` is a [UTCTimestamp](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp)
 number, false otherwise.

*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/isUTCTimestamp#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/isUTCTimestamp#returns)

# Function: createChartEx() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **createChartEx**<`HorzScaleItem`, `THorzScaleBehavior`\>(`container`, `horzScaleBehavior`, `options`?): [`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

This function is the main entry point of the Lightweight Charting Library. If you are using time values for the horizontal scale then it is recommended that you rather use the [createChart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart)
 function.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

type of points on the horizontal scale

• **THorzScaleBehavior** _extends_ [`IHorzScaleBehavior`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

type of horizontal axis strategy that encapsulate all the specific behaviors of the horizontal scale type

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **container**: `string` | `HTMLElement`

ID of HTML element or element itself

• **horzScaleBehavior**: `THorzScaleBehavior`

Horizontal scale behavior

• **options?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`ReturnType`<`THorzScaleBehavior`\[`"options"`\]>>

Any subset of options to be applied at start.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#returns "Direct link to Returns")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

An interface to the created chart

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#type-parameters)
    
*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx#returns)

---

# Function: defaultHorzScaleBehavior() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/defaultHorzScaleBehavior#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **defaultHorzScaleBehavior**(): () => [`IHorzScaleBehavior`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
>  <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
> \>

Provides the default implementation of the horizontal scale (time-based) that can be used as a base for extending the horizontal scale with custom behavior. This allows for the introduction of custom functionality without re-implementing the entire [IHorzScaleBehavior](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
<[Time](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\> interface.

For further details, refer to the [createChartEx](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChartEx)
 chart constructor method.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/defaultHorzScaleBehavior#returns "Direct link to Returns")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Function`

An uninitialized class implementing the [IHorzScaleBehavior](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
<[Time](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\> interface

### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/defaultHorzScaleBehavior#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IHorzScaleBehavior`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
 <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\>

*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/defaultHorzScaleBehavior#returns)
    *   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/defaultHorzScaleBehavior#returns-1)

---

# Function: createOptionsChart() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createOptionsChart#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **createOptionsChart**(`container`, `options`?): [`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
> <`number`\>

Creates an 'options' chart with price values on the horizontal scale.

This function is used to create a specialized chart type where the horizontal scale represents price values instead of time. It's particularly useful for visualizing option chains, price distributions, or any data where price is the primary x-axis metric.

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createOptionsChart#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **container**: `string` | `HTMLElement`

The DOM element or its id where the chart will be rendered.

• **options?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`PriceChartOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions)
\>

Optional configuration options for the price chart.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createOptionsChart#returns "Direct link to Returns")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
<`number`\>

An instance of IChartApiBase configured for price-based horizontal scaling.

*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createOptionsChart#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createOptionsChart#returns)

---

# Function: createYieldCurveChart() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createYieldCurveChart#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **createYieldCurveChart**(`container`, `options`?): [`IYieldCurveChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi)

Creates a yield curve chart with the specified options.

A yield curve chart differs from the default chart type in the following ways:

*   Horizontal scale is linearly spaced, and defined in monthly time duration units
*   Whitespace is ignored for the crosshair and grid lines

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createYieldCurveChart#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **container**: `string` | `HTMLElement`

ID of HTML element or element itself

• **options?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`YieldCurveChartOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions)
\>

The yield chart options.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createYieldCurveChart#returns "Direct link to Returns")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`IYieldCurveChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi)

An interface to the created chart

*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createYieldCurveChart#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createYieldCurveChart#returns)

---

# Function: createImageWatermark() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **createImageWatermark**<`T`\>(`pane`, `imageUrl`, `options`): [`IImageWatermarkPluginApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/IImageWatermarkPluginApi)
> <`T`\>

Creates an image watermark.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **T**

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **pane**: [`IPaneApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi)
<`T`\>

Target pane.

• **imageUrl**: `string`

Image URL.

• **options**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`ImageWatermarkOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions)
\>

Watermark options.

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#returns "Direct link to Returns")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`IImageWatermarkPluginApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/IImageWatermarkPluginApi)
<`T`\>

Image watermark wrapper.

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    import { createImageWatermark } from 'lightweight-charts';const firstPane = chart.panes()[0];const imageWatermark = createImageWatermark(firstPane, '/images/my-image.png', {  alpha: 0.5,  padding: 20,});// to change optionsimageWatermark.applyOptions({ padding: 10 });// to remove watermark from the paneimageWatermark.detach();

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#type-parameters)
    
*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#returns)
    
*   [Example](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createImageWatermark#example)

---

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createTextWatermark#example "Direct link to Example")

    import { createTextWatermark } from 'lightweight-charts';const firstPane = chart.panes()[0];const textWatermark = createTextWatermark(firstPane, {      horzAlign: 'center',      vertAlign: 'center',      lines: [        {          text: 'Hello',          color: 'rgba(255,0,0,0.5)',          fontSize: 100,          fontStyle: 'bold',        },        {          text: 'This is a text watermark',          color: 'rgba(0,0,255,0.5)',          fontSize: 50,          fontStyle: 'italic',          fontFamily: 'monospace',        },      ],});// to change optionstextWatermark.applyOptions({ horzAlign: 'left' });// to remove watermark from the panetextWatermark.detach();

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createTextWatermark#type-parameters)
    
*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createTextWatermark#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createTextWatermark#returns)
    
*   [Example](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createTextWatermark#example)

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library that is not designed to work on the server side, for example, with Node.js.

The library code targets the [_ES2020_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/11.0/)
. Therefore, the browsers you work with should support this language revision. Consider the following [table](https://compat-table.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
 to ensure the browser compatibility.

To support previous revisions, you can set up a transpilation process for the `lightweight-charts` package in your build system using tools such as Babel. If you encounter any issues, open a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/issues)
 with detailed information, and we will investigate potential solutions.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#installation "Direct link to Installation")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To set up the library, install the [`lightweight-charts`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/lightweight-charts)
 npm package:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

The package includes TypeScript declarations, enabling seamless integration within TypeScript projects.

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Lightweight Charts™ license **requires** specifying TradingView as the product creator. You should add the following attributes to a public page of your website or mobile application:

*   Attribution notice from the [`NOTICE`](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
     file
*   The [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
     link

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a first step, import the library to your file:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

To create a chart, use the [`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/functions/createChart)
 function. You can call the function multiple times to create as many charts as needed:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...const firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

As a result, `createChart` returns an [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object that allows you to interact with the created chart.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the chart is created, you can display data on it.

The basic primitive to display data is a [series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. The library supports the following series types:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series, use the [`addSeries`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#addseries)
 method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. As a parameter, specify a [series type](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/series-types)
 you would like to create:

    import { AreaSeries, BarSeries, BaselineSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);const barSeries = chart.addSeries(BarSeries);const baselineSeries = chart.addSeries(BaselineSeries);// ...

Note that a series **cannot be transferred** from one type to another one, since different series types require different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the series is created, you can populate it with data. Note that the API calls remain the same regardless of the series type, although the data format may vary.

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data to a series, you should call the [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: 'black', background: { type: 'solid', color: 'white' } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries, {    lineColor: '#2962FF', topColor: '#2962FF',    bottomColor: 'rgba(41, 98, 255, 0.28)',});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries, {    upColor: '#26a69a', downColor: '#ef5350', borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: '#26a69a', wickDownColor: '#ef5350',});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

You can also use `setData` to replace all data items.

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

If your data is updated, for example in real-time, you may also need to refresh the chart accordingly. To do this, call the [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
 method that allows you to update the last data item or add a new one.

    import { AreaSeries, CandlestickSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);areaSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries);candlestickSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// ...// Update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// Creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

We do not recommend calling [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 to update the chart, as this method replaces all series data and can significantly affect the performance.

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    import { createUpDownMarkers, createChart, LineSeries } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart('container');const lineSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries);const upDownMarkers = createUpDownMarkers(lineSeries, {    positiveColor: '#22AB94',    negativeColor: '#F7525F',    updateVisibilityDuration: 5000,});// to add some dataupDownMarkers.setData(    [        { time: '2020-02-02', value: 12.34 },        //... more line series data    ]);// ... Update some valuesupDownMarkers.update({ time: '2020-02-02', value: 13.54 }, true);// to remove plugin from the seriesupDownMarkers.detach();

    import { createSeriesMarkers } from 'lightweight-charts';    const seriesMarkers = createSeriesMarkers(        series,        [            {                color: 'green',                position: 'inBar',                shape: 'arrowDown',                time: 1556880900,            },        ]    ); // and then you can modify the markers // set it to empty array to remove all markers seriesMarkers.setMarkers([]); // `seriesMarkers.markers()` returns current markers

# Type alias: AlphaComponent | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/AlphaComponent#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

> **AlphaComponent**: [`Nominal`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Nominal)

Optional line color value for certain data item. If missed, color from options is used

> `optional` **topColor**: `string`

### bottomColor?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaData#bottomcolor "Direct link to bottomColor?")

> `optional` **bottomColor**: `string`

Optional bottom color value for certain data item. If missed, color from options is used

* * *

> **time**: `HorzScaleItem`

The time of the data.

Additional custom values which will be ignored by the library, but could be used by plugins.

# Type alias: CustomSeriesPricePlotValues | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesPricePlotValues#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

> **CustomSeriesPricePlotValues**: `number`\[\]

Price values for the custom series. This list should include the largest, smallest, and current price values for the data point. The last value in the array will be used for the current value. You shouldn't need to have more than 3 values in this array since the library only needs a largest, smallest, and current value.

Examples:

*   For a line series, this would contain a single number representing the current value.
*   For a candle series, this would contain the high, low, and close values. Where the last value would be the close value.

---

### upColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#upcolor "Direct link to upColor")

> **upColor**: `string`

Color of rising bars.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

* * *

### downColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#downcolor "Direct link to downColor")

> **downColor**: `string`

Color of falling bars.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#ef5350'`

* * *

### openVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#openvisible "Direct link to openVisible")

> **openVisible**: `boolean`

Show open lines on bars.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### thinBars[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#thinbars "Direct link to thinBars")

> **thinBars**: `boolean`

Show bars as sticks.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

# Type alias: LogicalRange | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LogicalRange#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

> **LogicalRange**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
>  <[`Logical`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Logical)
> \>

A logical range is an object with 2 properties: `from` and `to`, which are numbers and represent logical indexes on the time scale.

The starting point of the time scale's logical range is the first data item among all series. Before that point all indexes are negative, starting from that point - positive.

Indexes might have fractional parts, for instance 4.2, due to the time-scale being continuous rather than discrete.

Integer part of the logical index means index of the fully visible bar. Thus, if we have 5.2 as the last visible logical index (`to` field), that means that the last visible bar has index 5, but we also have partially visible (for 20%) 6th bar. Half (e.g. 1.5, 3.5, 10.5) means exactly a middle of the bar.

---

# Interface: BarsInfo\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents a range of bars and the number of bars outside the range.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#extends "Direct link to Extends")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `Partial` <[`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
    <`HorzScaleItem`\>>

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### barsBefore[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#barsbefore "Direct link to barsBefore")

> **barsBefore**: `number`

The number of bars before the start of the range. Positive value means that there are some bars before (out of logical range from the left) the [IRange.from](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange#from)
 logical index in the series. Negative value means that the first series' bar is inside the passed logical range, and between the first series' bar and the [IRange.from](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange#from)
 logical index are some bars.

* * *

### barsAfter[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#barsafter "Direct link to barsAfter")

> **barsAfter**: `number`

The number of bars after the end of the range. Positive value in the `barsAfter` field means that there are some bars after (out of logical range from the right) the [IRange.to](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange#to)
 logical index in the series. Negative value means that the last series' bar is inside the passed logical range, and between the last series' bar and the [IRange.to](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange#to)
 logical index are some bars.

* * *

### from?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#from "Direct link to from?")

> `optional` **from**: `HorzScaleItem`

The from value. The start of the range.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Partial.from`

* * *

### to?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#to "Direct link to to?")

> `optional` **to**: `HorzScaleItem`

The to value. The end of the range.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Partial.to`

*   [Extends](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#extends)
    
*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#type-parameters)
    
*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#properties)
    *   [barsBefore](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo#barsbefore)

# Type alias: PriceToCoordinateConverter() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **PriceToCoordinateConverter**: (`price`) => [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)
>  | `null`

Converter function for changing prices into vertical coordinate values.

This is provided as a convenience function since the series original data will most likely be defined in price values, and the renderer needs to draw with coordinates. This returns the same values as directly using the series' priceToCoordinate method.

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **price**: `number`

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter#returns "Direct link to Returns")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)
 | `null`

*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter#returns)

# Interface: CandlestickStyleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents style options for a candlestick series.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### upColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#upcolor "Direct link to upColor")

> **upColor**: `string`

Color of rising candles.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#26a69a'`

* * *

### downColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#downcolor "Direct link to downColor")

> **downColor**: `string`

Color of falling candles.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#ef5350'`

* * *

### wickVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#wickvisible "Direct link to wickVisible")

> **wickVisible**: `boolean`

Enable high and low prices candle wicks.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### borderVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#bordervisible "Direct link to borderVisible")

> **borderVisible**: `boolean`

Enable candle borders.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### borderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#bordercolor "Direct link to borderColor")

> **borderColor**: `string`

Border color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#378658'`

* * *

### borderUpColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#borderupcolor "Direct link to borderUpColor")

> **borderUpColor**: `string`

Border color of rising candles.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#26a69a'`

* * *

### borderDownColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#borderdowncolor "Direct link to borderDownColor")

> **borderDownColor**: `string`

Border color of falling candles.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#ef5350'`

* * *

### wickColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#wickcolor "Direct link to wickColor")

> **wickColor**: `string`

Wick color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#737375'`

* * *

### wickUpColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#wickupcolor "Direct link to wickUpColor")

> **wickUpColor**: `string`

Wick color of rising candles.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#26a69a'`

* * *

### wickDownColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#wickdowncolor "Direct link to wickDownColor")

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#ef5350'`

# Type alias: UTCTimestamp | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **UTCTimestamp**: [`Nominal`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Nominal)
> <`number`, `"UTCTimestamp"`\>

Represents a time as a UNIX timestamp.

If your chart displays an intraday interval you should use a UNIX Timestamp.

Note that JavaScript Date APIs like `Date.now` return a number of milliseconds but UTCTimestamp expects a number of seconds.

Note that to prevent errors, you should cast the numeric type of the time to `UTCTimestamp` type from the package (`value as UTCTimestamp`) in TypeScript code.

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const timestamp = 1529899200 as UTCTimestamp; // Literal timestamp representing 2018-06-25T04:00:00.000Zconst timestamp2 = (Date.now() / 1000) as UTCTimestamp;

*   [Example](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp#example)

---

# Interface: AreaStyleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents style options for an area series.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### topColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#topcolor "Direct link to topColor")

> **topColor**: `string`

Color of the top part of the area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba( 46, 220, 135, 0.4)'`

* * *

### bottomColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#bottomcolor "Direct link to bottomColor")

> **bottomColor**: `string`

Color of the bottom part of the area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba( 40, 221, 100, 0)'`

* * *

### relativeGradient[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#relativegradient "Direct link to relativeGradient")

> **relativeGradient**: `boolean`

Gradient is relative to the base value and the currently visible range. If it is false, the gradient is relative to the top and bottom of the chart.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### invertFilledArea[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#invertfilledarea "Direct link to invertFilledArea")

> **invertFilledArea**: `boolean`

Invert the filled area. Fills the area above the line if set to true.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### lineColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#linecolor "Direct link to lineColor")

> **lineColor**: `string`

Line color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#33D778'`

* * *

### lineStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#linestyle "Direct link to lineStyle")

> **lineStyle**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Line style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.Solid}

* * *

### lineWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#linewidth "Direct link to lineWidth")

> **lineWidth**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Line width in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`3`

* * *

### lineType[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#linetype "Direct link to lineType")

> **lineType**: [`LineType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineType)

Line type.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineType.Simple}

* * *

### lineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#linevisible "Direct link to lineVisible")

> **lineVisible**: `boolean`

Show series line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### pointMarkersVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#pointmarkersvisible "Direct link to pointMarkersVisible")

> **pointMarkersVisible**: `boolean`

Show circle markers on each point.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### pointMarkersRadius?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#pointmarkersradius "Direct link to pointMarkersRadius?")

> `optional` **pointMarkersRadius**: `number`

Circle markers radius in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible "Direct link to crosshairMarkerVisible")

> **crosshairMarkerVisible**: `boolean`

Show the crosshair marker.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerRadius[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerradius "Direct link to crosshairMarkerRadius")

> **crosshairMarkerRadius**: `number`

Crosshair marker radius in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

`4`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBorderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbordercolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderColor**: `string`

Crosshair marker border color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-13 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbackgroundcolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor**: `string`

The crosshair marker background color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-14 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBorderWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerborderwidth "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderWidth")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderWidth**: `number`

Crosshair marker border width in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-15 "Direct link to Default Value")

`2`

* * *

### lastPriceAnimation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#lastpriceanimation "Direct link to lastPriceAnimation")

> **lastPriceAnimation**: [`LastPriceAnimationMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode)

Last price animation mode.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#default-value-16 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LastPriceAnimationMode.Disabled}

# Type alias: TickMarkFormatter() | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **TickMarkFormatter**: (`time`, `tickMarkType`, `locale`) => `string` | `null`

The `TickMarkFormatter` is used to customize tick mark labels on the time scale.

This function should return `time` as a string formatted according to `tickMarkType` type (year, month, etc) and `locale`.

Note that the returned string should be the shortest possible value and should have no more than 8 characters. Otherwise, the tick marks will overlap each other.

If the formatter function returns `null` then the default tick mark formatter will be used as a fallback.

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#example "Direct link to Example")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const customFormatter = (time, tickMarkType, locale) => {    // your code here};

Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **time**: [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

• **tickMarkType**: [`TickMarkType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TickMarkType)

• **locale**: `string`

Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#returns "Direct link to Returns")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`string` | `null`

*   [Example](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#example)
    
*   [Parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#parameters)
    
*   [Returns](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter#returns)

# Type alias: Time | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

> **Time**: [`UTCTimestamp`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp)
>  | [`BusinessDay`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BusinessDay)
>  | `string`

The Time type is used to represent the time of data items.

Values can be a [UTCTimestamp](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/UTCTimestamp)
, a [BusinessDay](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BusinessDay)
, or a business day string in ISO format.

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time#example "Direct link to Example")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const timestamp = 1529899200; // Literal timestamp representing 2018-06-25T04:00:00.000Zconst businessDay = { year: 2019, month: 6, day: 1 }; // June 1, 2019const businessDayString = '2021-02-03'; // Business day string literal

*   [Example](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time#example)

---

# Type alias: PrimitivePaneViewZOrder | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PrimitivePaneViewZOrder#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

> **PrimitivePaneViewZOrder**: `"bottom"` | `"normal"` | `"top"`

Defines where in the visual layer stack the renderer should be executed.

*   `bottom`: Draw below everything except the background.
*   `normal`: Draw at the same level as the series.
*   `top`: Draw above everything (including the crosshair).

---

# Interface: CrosshairOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Structure describing crosshair options

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### mode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#mode "Direct link to mode")

> **mode**: [`CrosshairMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/CrosshairMode)

Crosshair mode

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link CrosshairMode.Magnet}

* * *

### vertLine[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#vertline "Direct link to vertLine")

> **vertLine**: [`CrosshairLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions)

Vertical line options.

* * *

### horzLine[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#horzline "Direct link to horzLine")

> **horzLine**: [`CrosshairLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions)

Horizontal line options.

* * *

### doNotSnapToHiddenSeriesIndices[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#donotsnaptohiddenseriesindices "Direct link to doNotSnapToHiddenSeriesIndices")

> **doNotSnapToHiddenSeriesIndices**: `boolean`

If set to `true`, the crosshair will not snap to the data points of hidden series.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

# Type alias: SeriesMarkerZOrder | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/SeriesMarkerZOrder#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

> **SeriesMarkerZOrder**: `"top"` | `"aboveSeries"` | `"normal"`

The visual stacking order for the markers within the chart.

*   `normal`: Markers are drawn together with the series they belong to. They can appear below other series depending on the series stacking order.
*   `aboveSeries`: Markers are drawn above all series but below primitives that use the 'top' zOrder layer.
*   `top`: Markers are drawn on the topmost primitive layer, above all series and (most) other primitives.

---

# Interface: CrosshairLineOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Structure describing a crosshair line (vertical or horizontal)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#color "Direct link to color")

> **color**: `string`

Crosshair line color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#758696'`

* * *

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Crosshair line width.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`1`

* * *

### style[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#style "Direct link to style")

> **style**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Crosshair line style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.LargeDashed}

* * *

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Display the crosshair line.

Note that disabling crosshair lines does not disable crosshair marker on Line and Area series. It can be disabled by using `crosshairMarkerVisible` option of a relevant series.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#see "Direct link to See")

*   [LineStyleOptions.crosshairMarkerVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible)
    
*   [AreaStyleOptions.crosshairMarkerVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible)
    
*   [BaselineStyleOptions.crosshairMarkerVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible)
    

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### labelVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#labelvisible "Direct link to labelVisible")

> **labelVisible**: `boolean`

Display the crosshair label on the relevant scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### labelBackgroundColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#labelbackgroundcolor "Direct link to labelBackgroundColor")

> **labelBackgroundColor**: `string`

Crosshair label background color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#4c525e'`

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#properties)
    *   [color](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#color)
        
    *   [width](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#width)
        
    *   [style](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairLineOptions#style)

# Interface: HandleScaleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents options for how the chart is scaled by the mouse and touch gestures.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### mouseWheel[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#mousewheel "Direct link to mouseWheel")

> **mouseWheel**: `boolean`

Enable scaling with the mouse wheel.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### pinch[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#pinch "Direct link to pinch")

> **pinch**: `boolean`

Enable scaling with pinch/zoom gestures.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### axisPressedMouseMove[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#axispressedmousemove "Direct link to axisPressedMouseMove")

> **axisPressedMouseMove**: `boolean` | [`AxisPressedMouseMoveOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AxisPressedMouseMoveOptions)

Enable scaling the price and/or time scales by holding down the left mouse button and moving the mouse.

* * *

### axisDoubleClickReset[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#axisdoubleclickreset "Direct link to axisDoubleClickReset")

> **axisDoubleClickReset**: `boolean` | [`AxisDoubleClickOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AxisDoubleClickOptions)

Enable resetting scaling by double-clicking the left mouse button.

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#properties)
    *   [mouseWheel](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions#mousewheel)
