# Interface: HandleScrollOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents options for how the chart is scrolled by the mouse and touch gestures.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### mouseWheel[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#mousewheel "Direct link to mouseWheel")

> **mouseWheel**: `boolean`

Enable scrolling with the mouse wheel.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### pressedMouseMove[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#pressedmousemove "Direct link to pressedMouseMove")

> **pressedMouseMove**: `boolean`

Enable scrolling by holding down the left mouse button and moving the mouse.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### horzTouchDrag[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#horztouchdrag "Direct link to horzTouchDrag")

> **horzTouchDrag**: `boolean`

Enable horizontal touch scrolling.

When enabled the chart handles touch gestures that would normally scroll the webpage horizontally.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### vertTouchDrag[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#verttouchdrag "Direct link to vertTouchDrag")

> **vertTouchDrag**: `boolean`

Enable vertical touch scrolling.

When enabled the chart handles touch gestures that would normally scroll the webpage vertically.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

# Interface: ChartOptionsBase | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents common chart options

Extended by[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#extended-by "Direct link to Extended by")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
    

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: `number`

Width of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

* * *

### height[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#height "Direct link to height")

> **height**: `number`

Height of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

* * *

### autoSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#autosize "Direct link to autoSize")

> **autoSize**: `boolean`

Setting this flag to `true` will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes.

This feature requires [`ResizeObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver)
 class to be available in the global scope. Note that calling code is responsible for providing a polyfill if required. If the global scope does not have `ResizeObserver`, a warning will appear and the flag will be ignored.

Please pay attention that `autoSize` option and explicit sizes options `width` and `height` don't conflict with one another. If you specify `autoSize` flag, then `width` and `height` options will be ignored unless `ResizeObserver` has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag `autoSize` could also be set with and unset with `applyOptions` function.

    const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, {    autoSize: true,});

* * *

### layout[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#layout "Direct link to layout")

> **layout**: [`LayoutOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions)

Layout options

* * *

### leftPriceScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#leftpricescale "Direct link to leftPriceScale")

> **leftPriceScale**: [`PriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions)

Left price scale options

* * *

### rightPriceScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#rightpricescale "Direct link to rightPriceScale")

> **rightPriceScale**: [`PriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions)

Right price scale options

* * *

### overlayPriceScales[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#overlaypricescales "Direct link to overlayPriceScales")

> **overlayPriceScales**: [`OverlayPriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/OverlayPriceScaleOptions)

Overlay price scale options

* * *

### timeScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#timescale "Direct link to timeScale")

> **timeScale**: [`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)

Time scale options

* * *

### crosshair[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#crosshair "Direct link to crosshair")

> **crosshair**: [`CrosshairOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CrosshairOptions)

The crosshair shows the intersection of the price and time scale values at any point on the chart.

* * *

### grid[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#grid "Direct link to grid")

> **grid**: [`GridOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/GridOptions)

A grid is represented in the chart background as a vertical and horizontal lines drawn at the levels of visible marks of price and the time scales.

* * *

### handleScroll[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#handlescroll "Direct link to handleScroll")

> **handleScroll**: `boolean` | [`HandleScrollOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions)

Scroll options, or a boolean flag that enables/disables scrolling

* * *

### handleScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#handlescale "Direct link to handleScale")

> **handleScale**: `boolean` | [`HandleScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions)

Scale options, or a boolean flag that enables/disables scaling

* * *

### kineticScroll[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#kineticscroll "Direct link to kineticScroll")

> **kineticScroll**: [`KineticScrollOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/KineticScrollOptions)

Kinetic scroll options

* * *

### trackingMode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#trackingmode "Direct link to trackingMode")

> **trackingMode**: [`TrackingModeOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions)

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

* * *

### localization[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#localization "Direct link to localization")

> **localization**: [`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)

Basic localization options

* * *

### addDefaultPane[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#adddefaultpane "Direct link to addDefaultPane")

> **addDefaultPane**: `boolean`

Whether to add a default pane to the chart Disable this option when you want to create a chart with no panes and add them manually

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

# Interface: BaselineStyleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents style options for a baseline series.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### baseValue[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#basevalue "Direct link to baseValue")

> **baseValue**: [`BaseValuePrice`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaseValuePrice)

Base value of the series.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`{ type: 'price', price: 0 }`

* * *

### relativeGradient[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#relativegradient "Direct link to relativeGradient")

> **relativeGradient**: `boolean`

Gradient is relative to the base value and the currently visible range. If it is false, the gradient is relative to the top and bottom of the chart.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### topFillColor1[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#topfillcolor1 "Direct link to topFillColor1")

> **topFillColor1**: `string`

The first color of the top area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(38, 166, 154, 0.28)'`

* * *

### topFillColor2[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#topfillcolor2 "Direct link to topFillColor2")

> **topFillColor2**: `string`

The second color of the top area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(38, 166, 154, 0.05)'`

* * *

### topLineColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#toplinecolor "Direct link to topLineColor")

> **topLineColor**: `string`

The line color of the top area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(38, 166, 154, 1)'`

* * *

### bottomFillColor1[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#bottomfillcolor1 "Direct link to bottomFillColor1")

> **bottomFillColor1**: `string`

The first color of the bottom area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(239, 83, 80, 0.05)'`

* * *

### bottomFillColor2[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#bottomfillcolor2 "Direct link to bottomFillColor2")

> **bottomFillColor2**: `string`

The second color of the bottom area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(239, 83, 80, 0.28)'`

* * *

### bottomLineColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#bottomlinecolor "Direct link to bottomLineColor")

> **bottomLineColor**: `string`

The line color of the bottom area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(239, 83, 80, 1)'`

* * *

### lineWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#linewidth "Direct link to lineWidth")

> **lineWidth**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Line width.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`3`

* * *

### lineStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#linestyle "Direct link to lineStyle")

> **lineStyle**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Line style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.Solid}

* * *

### lineType[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#linetype "Direct link to lineType")

> **lineType**: [`LineType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineType)

Line type.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineType.Simple}

* * *

### lineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#linevisible "Direct link to lineVisible")

> **lineVisible**: `boolean`

Show series line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### pointMarkersVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#pointmarkersvisible "Direct link to pointMarkersVisible")

> **pointMarkersVisible**: `boolean`

Show circle markers on each point.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### pointMarkersRadius?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#pointmarkersradius "Direct link to pointMarkersRadius?")

> `optional` **pointMarkersRadius**: `number`

Circle markers radius in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-13 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible "Direct link to crosshairMarkerVisible")

> **crosshairMarkerVisible**: `boolean`

Show the crosshair marker.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-14 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerRadius[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerradius "Direct link to crosshairMarkerRadius")

> **crosshairMarkerRadius**: `number`

Crosshair marker radius in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-15 "Direct link to Default Value")

`4`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBorderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbordercolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderColor**: `string`

Crosshair marker border color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-16 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbackgroundcolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor**: `string`

The crosshair marker background color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-17 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBorderWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerborderwidth "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderWidth")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderWidth**: `number`

Crosshair marker border width in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-18 "Direct link to Default Value")

`2`

* * *

### lastPriceAnimation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#lastpriceanimation "Direct link to lastPriceAnimation")

> **lastPriceAnimation**: [`LastPriceAnimationMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode)

Last price animation mode.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions#default-value-19 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LastPriceAnimationMode.Disabled}

# Interface: CustomData\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Base structure describing a single item of data for a custom series.

This type allows for any properties to be defined within the interface. It is recommended that you extend this interface with the required data structure.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#extends "Direct link to Extends")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`CustomSeriesWhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomSeriesWhitespaceData)
    <`HorzScaleItem`\>

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#color "Direct link to color?")

> `optional` **color**: `string`

If defined then this color will be used for the price line and price scale line for this specific data item of the custom series.

* * *

### time[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#time "Direct link to time")

> **time**: `HorzScaleItem`

The time of the data.

[`CustomSeriesWhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomSeriesWhitespaceData)
 . [`time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomSeriesWhitespaceData#time)

* * *

### customValues?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData#customvalues "Direct link to customValues?")

> `optional` **customValues**: `Record`<`string`, `unknown`\>

Additional custom values which will be ignored by the library, but could be used by plugins.

 . [`customValues`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomSeriesWhitespaceData#customvalues)

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: `number`

Width of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase)
 . [`width`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#width)

* * *

### height[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#height "Direct link to height")

> **height**: `number`

Height of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase)
 . [`height`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#height)

* * *

### autoSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#autosize "Direct link to autoSize")

> **autoSize**: `boolean`

Setting this flag to `true` will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes.

This feature requires [`ResizeObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver)
 class to be available in the global scope. Note that calling code is responsible for providing a polyfill if required. If the global scope does not have `ResizeObserver`, a warning will appear and the flag will be ignored.

Please pay attention that `autoSize` option and explicit sizes options `width` and `height` don't conflict with one another. If you specify `autoSize` flag, then `width` and `height` options will be ignored unless `ResizeObserver` has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag `autoSize` could also be set with and unset with `applyOptions` function.

    const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, {    autoSize: true,});

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase)
 . [`autoSize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsBase#autosize)

* * *

### trackingMode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#trackingmode "Direct link to trackingMode")

> **trackingMode**: [`TrackingModeOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions)

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

# Interface: IPriceLine | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the interface for interacting with price lines.

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### applyOptions()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#applyoptions "Direct link to applyOptions()")

> **applyOptions**(`options`): `void`

Apply options to the price line.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **options**: `Partial` <[`PriceLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions)
\>

Any subset of options.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#example "Direct link to Example")

    priceLine.applyOptions({    price: 90.0,    color: 'red',    lineWidth: 3,    lineStyle: LightweightCharts.LineStyle.Dashed,    axisLabelVisible: false,    title: 'P/L 600',});

* * *

### options()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#options "Direct link to options()")

> **options**(): `Readonly` <[`PriceLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions)
> \>

Get the currently applied options.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`Readonly` <[`PriceLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions)
\>

Version: 5.1

On this page

Interface to control chart's price scale

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### applyOptions()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#applyoptions "Direct link to applyOptions()")

> **applyOptions**(`options`): `void`

Applies new options to the price scale

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **options**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`PriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions)
\>

Any subset of options.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### options()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#options "Direct link to options()")

> **options**(): `Readonly` <[`PriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions)
> \>

Returns currently applied options of the price scale

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`Readonly` <[`PriceScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions)
\>

Full set of currently applied options, including defaults

* * *

### width()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#width "Direct link to width()")

> **width**(): `number`

Returns a width of the price scale if it's visible or 0 if invisible.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

* * *

### setVisibleRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#setvisiblerange "Direct link to setVisibleRange()")

> **setVisibleRange**(`range`): `void`

Sets the visible range of the price scale.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **range**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`number`\>

The visible range to set, with `from` and `to` properties.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getVisibleRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#getvisiblerange "Direct link to getVisibleRange()")

> **getVisibleRange**(): [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
> <`number`\>

Returns the visible range of the price scale.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`number`\>

The visible range of the price scale, or null if the range is not set.

* * *

### setAutoScale()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#setautoscale "Direct link to setAutoScale()")

> **setAutoScale**(`on`): `void`

Sets the auto scale mode of the price scale.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **on**: `boolean`

If true, enables auto scaling; if false, disables it.

`void`

On this page

Renderer for the custom series. This paints on the main chart pane.

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneRenderer#methods "Direct link to Methods")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### draw()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneRenderer#draw "Direct link to draw()")

• **target**: `CanvasRenderingTarget2D`

canvas context to draw on, refer to FancyCanvas library for more details about this class.

• **priceConverter**: [`PriceToCoordinateConverter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceToCoordinateConverter)

converter function for changing prices into vertical coordinate values.

• **isHovered**: `boolean`

Whether the series is hovered.

• **hitTestData?**: `unknown`

Optional hit test data for the series.

# Interface: HorzScaleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Options for the time scale; the horizontal scale at the bottom of the chart that displays the time of data.

Extended by[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#extended-by "Direct link to Extended by")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`TimeScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions)
    

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### rightOffset[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightoffset "Direct link to rightOffset")

> **rightOffset**: `number`

The margin space in bars from the right side of the chart.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`0`

* * *

### rightOffsetPixels?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightoffsetpixels "Direct link to rightOffsetPixels?")

> `optional` **rightOffsetPixels**: `number`

The margin space in pixels from the right side of the chart. This option has priority over `rightOffset`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### barSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#barspacing "Direct link to barSpacing")

> **barSpacing**: `number`

The space between bars in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`6`

* * *

### minBarSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#minbarspacing "Direct link to minBarSpacing")

> **minBarSpacing**: `number`

The minimum space between bars in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`0.5`

* * *

### maxBarSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#maxbarspacing "Direct link to maxBarSpacing")

> **maxBarSpacing**: `number`

The maximum space between bars in pixels.

Has no effect if value is set to `0`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`0`

* * *

### fixLeftEdge[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#fixleftedge "Direct link to fixLeftEdge")

> **fixLeftEdge**: `boolean`

Prevent scrolling to the left of the first bar.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### fixRightEdge[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#fixrightedge "Direct link to fixRightEdge")

> **fixRightEdge**: `boolean`

Prevent scrolling to the right of the most recent bar.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#lockvisibletimerangeonresize "Direct link to lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize")

> **lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize**: `boolean`

Prevent changing the visible time range during chart resizing.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### rightBarStaysOnScroll[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightbarstaysonscroll "Direct link to rightBarStaysOnScroll")

> **rightBarStaysOnScroll**: `boolean`

Prevent the hovered bar from moving when scrolling.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### borderVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#bordervisible "Direct link to borderVisible")

> **borderVisible**: `boolean`

Show the time scale border.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### borderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#bordercolor "Direct link to borderColor")

> **borderColor**: `string`

The time scale border color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#2B2B43'`

* * *

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Show the time scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### timeVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#timevisible "Direct link to timeVisible")

> **timeVisible**: `boolean`

Show the time, not just the date, in the time scale and vertical crosshair label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### secondsVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#secondsvisible "Direct link to secondsVisible")

> **secondsVisible**: `boolean`

Show seconds in the time scale and vertical crosshair label in `hh:mm:ss` format for intraday data.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-13 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#shiftvisiblerangeonnewbar "Direct link to shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar")

> **shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar**: `boolean`

Shift the visible range to the right (into the future) by the number of new bars when new data is added.

Note that this only applies when the last bar is visible.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-14 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#allowshiftvisiblerangeonwhitespacereplacement "Direct link to allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement")

> **allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement**: `boolean`

Allow the visible range to be shifted to the right when a new bar is added which is replacing an existing whitespace time point on the chart.

Note that this only applies when the last bar is visible & `shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar` is enabled.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-15 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### ticksVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#ticksvisible "Direct link to ticksVisible")

> **ticksVisible**: `boolean`

Draw small vertical line on time axis labels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-16 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### tickMarkMaxCharacterLength?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#tickmarkmaxcharacterlength "Direct link to tickMarkMaxCharacterLength?")

> `optional` **tickMarkMaxCharacterLength**: `number`

Maximum tick mark label length. Used to override the default 8 character maximum length.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-17 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### uniformDistribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#uniformdistribution "Direct link to uniformDistribution")

> **uniformDistribution**: `boolean`

Changes horizontal scale marks generation. With this flag equal to `true`, marks of the same weight are either all drawn or none are drawn at all.

* * *

### minimumHeight[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#minimumheight "Direct link to minimumHeight")

> **minimumHeight**: `number`

Define a minimum height for the time scale. Note: This value will be exceeded if the time scale needs more space to display it's contents.

Setting a minimum height could be useful for ensuring that multiple charts positioned in a horizontal stack each have an identical time scale height, or for plugins which require a bit more space within the time scale pane.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-18 "Direct link to Default Value")

    0

* * *

### allowBoldLabels[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#allowboldlabels "Direct link to allowBoldLabels")

> **allowBoldLabels**: `boolean`

Allow major time scale labels to be rendered in a bolder font weight.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-19 "Direct link to Default Value")

    true

* * *

### ignoreWhitespaceIndices[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#ignorewhitespaceindices "Direct link to ignoreWhitespaceIndices")

> **ignoreWhitespaceIndices**: `boolean`

Ignore time scale points containing only whitespace (for all series) when drawing grid lines, tick marks, and snapping the crosshair to time scale points.

For the yield curve chart type it defaults to `true`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-20 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

* * *

### enableConflation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#enableconflation "Direct link to enableConflation")

> **enableConflation**: `boolean`

Enable data conflation for performance optimization when bar spacing is very small. When enabled, multiple data points are automatically combined into single points when they would be rendered in less than 0.5 pixels of screen space. This significantly improves rendering performance for large datasets when zoomed out.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-21 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

* * *

### conflationThresholdFactor?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#conflationthresholdfactor "Direct link to conflationThresholdFactor?")

> `optional` **conflationThresholdFactor**: `number`

Smoothing factor for conflation thresholds. Controls how aggressively conflation is applied. This can be used to create smoother-looking charts, especially useful for sparklines and small charts.

*   1.0 = conflate only when display can't show detail (default, performance-focused)
*   2.0 = conflate at 2x the display threshold (moderate smoothing)
*   4.0 = conflate at 4x the display threshold (strong smoothing)
*   8.0+ = very aggressive smoothing for very small charts

Higher values result in fewer data points being displayed, creating smoother but less detailed charts. This is particularly useful for sparklines and small charts where smooth appearance is prioritized over showing every data point.

Note: Should be used with continuous series types (line, area, baseline) for best visual results. Candlestick and bar series may look less natural with high smoothing factors.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-22 "Direct link to Default Value")

    1.0

* * *

### precomputeConflationOnInit[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#precomputeconflationoninit "Direct link to precomputeConflationOnInit")

> **precomputeConflationOnInit**: `boolean`

Precompute conflation chunks for common levels right after data load. When enabled, the system will precompute conflation data in the background, which improves performance when zooming out but increases initial load time and memory usage.

Performance impact:

*   Initial load: +100-500ms depending on dataset size
*   Memory usage: +20-50% of original dataset size
*   Zoom performance: Significant improvement (10-100x faster)

Recommended for: Large datasets (>10K points) on machines with sufficient memory

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-23 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

* * *

### precomputeConflationPriority[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#precomputeconflationpriority "Direct link to precomputeConflationPriority")

> **precomputeConflationPriority**: `"background"` | `"user-visible"` | `"user-blocking"`

Priority used for background precompute tasks when the Prioritized Task Scheduling API is available.

Options:

*   'background': Lowest priority, tasks run only when the browser is idle
*   'user-visible': Medium priority, tasks run when they might affect visible content
*   'user-blocking': Highest priority, tasks run immediately and may block user interaction

Recommendation: Use 'background' for most cases to avoid impacting user experience. Only use higher priorities if conflation is critical for your application's functionality.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#default-value-24 "Direct link to Default Value")

    'background'

*   [Extended by](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#extended-by)

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Context object provided to custom series conflation reducers. This wraps the internal SeriesPlotRow data while providing a user-friendly interface.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<`HorzScaleItem`\>

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> `readonly` **data**: `TData`

The original custom data item provided by the user.

* * *

### index[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#index "Direct link to index")

> `readonly` **index**: `number`

The time index of the data point in the series.

* * *

### originalTime[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#originaltime "Direct link to originalTime")

> `readonly` **originalTime**: `HorzScaleItem`

The original time value provided by the user.

* * *

### time[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#time "Direct link to time")

> `readonly` **time**: `unknown`

The internal time point object.

* * *

### priceValues[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext#pricevalues "Direct link to priceValues")

> `readonly` **priceValues**: [`CustomSeriesPricePlotValues`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesPricePlotValues)

The computed price values for this data point (as returned by priceValueBuilder). The last value in this array is used as the current price.

# Interface: ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

This interface represents a label on the price or time axis

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### coordinate()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#coordinate "Direct link to coordinate()")

> **coordinate**(): `number`

The desired coordinate for the label. Note that the label will be automatically moved to prevent overlapping with other labels. If you would like the label to be drawn at the exact coordinate under all circumstances then rather use `fixedCoordinate`. For a price axis the value returned will represent the vertical distance (pixels) from the top. For a time axis the value will represent the horizontal distance from the left.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

coordinate. distance from top for price axis, or distance from left for time axis.

* * *

### fixedCoordinate()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#fixedcoordinate "Direct link to fixedCoordinate()?")

> `optional` **fixedCoordinate**(): `number`

fixed coordinate of the label. A label with a fixed coordinate value will always be drawn at the specified coordinate and will appear above any 'unfixed' labels. If you supply a fixed coordinate then you should return a large negative number for `coordinate` so that the automatic placement of unfixed labels doesn't leave a blank space for this label. For a price axis the value returned will represent the vertical distance (pixels) from the top. For a time axis the value will represent the horizontal distance from the left.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

coordinate. distance from top for price axis, or distance from left for time axis.

* * *

### text()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#text "Direct link to text()")

> **text**(): `string`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

text of the label

* * *

### textColor()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#textcolor "Direct link to textColor()")

> **textColor**(): `string`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

text color of the label

* * *

### backColor()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#backcolor "Direct link to backColor()")

> **backColor**(): `string`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

background color of the label

* * *

### visible()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#visible "Direct link to visible()?")

> `optional` **visible**(): `boolean`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`boolean`

whether the label should be visible (default: `true`)

* * *

### tickVisible()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#tickvisible "Direct link to tickVisible()?")

> `optional` **tickVisible**(): `boolean`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`boolean`

whether the tick mark line should be visible (default: `true`)

*   [Methods](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#methods)
    *   [coordinate()](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#coordinate)

    *   [backColor()](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#backcolor)
        
    *   [visible()?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView#visible)

# Interface: IPanePrimitiveBase\<TPaneAttachedParameters\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Base interface for series primitives. It must be implemented to add some external graphics to series

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **TPaneAttachedParameters** = `unknown`

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### updateAllViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#updateallviews "Direct link to updateAllViews()?")

> `optional` **updateAllViews**(): `void`

This method is called when viewport has been changed, so primitive have to recalculate / invalidate its data

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### paneViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#paneviews "Direct link to paneViews()?")

> `optional` **paneViews**(): readonly [`IPanePrimitivePaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitivePaneView)
> \[\]

Returns array of objects representing primitive in the main area of the chart

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

readonly [`IPanePrimitivePaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitivePaneView)
\[\]

array of objects; each of then must implement IPrimitivePaneView interface

For performance reasons, the lightweight library uses internal caches based on references to arrays So, this method must return new array if set of views has changed and should try to return the same array if nothing changed

* * *

### attached()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#attached "Direct link to attached()?")

> `optional` **attached**(`param`): `void`

Attached Lifecycle hook.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **param**: `TPaneAttachedParameters`

An object containing useful references for the attached primitive to use.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

void

* * *

### detached()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#detached "Direct link to detached()?")

> `optional` **detached**(): `void`

Detached Lifecycle hook.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

void

* * *

### hitTest()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#hittest "Direct link to hitTest()?")

> `optional` **hitTest**(`x`, `y`): [`PrimitiveHoveredItem`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem)

Hit test method which will be called by the library when the cursor is moved. Use this to register object ids being hovered for use within the crosshairMoved and click events emitted by the chart. Additionally, the hit test result can specify a preferred cursor type to display for the main chart pane. This method should return the top most hit for this primitive if more than one object is being intersected.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **x**: `number`

x Coordinate of mouse event

• **y**: `number`

y Coordinate of mouse event

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

[`PrimitiveHoveredItem`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem)

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#type-parameters)

    *   [updateAllViews()?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPanePrimitiveBase#updateallviews)

* * *

### priceValueBuilder()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#pricevaluebuilder "Direct link to priceValueBuilder()")

> **priceValueBuilder**(`plotRow`): [`CustomSeriesPricePlotValues`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesPricePlotValues)

A function for interpreting the custom series data and returning an array of numbers representing the price values for the item. These price values are used by the chart to determine the auto-scaling (to ensure the items are in view) and the crosshair and price line positions. The last value in the array will be used as the current value. You shouldn't need to have more than 3 values in this array since the library only needs a largest, smallest, and current value.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **plotRow**: `TData`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

[`CustomSeriesPricePlotValues`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesPricePlotValues)

* * *

### isWhitespace()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#iswhitespace "Direct link to isWhitespace()")

> **isWhitespace**(`data`): `data is CustomSeriesWhitespaceData<HorzScaleItem>`

A function for testing whether a data point should be considered fully specified, or if it should be considered as whitespace. Should return `true` if is whitespace.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **data**: `TData` | [`CustomSeriesWhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomSeriesWhitespaceData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

data point to be tested

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`data is CustomSeriesWhitespaceData<HorzScaleItem>`

* * *

### defaultOptions()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#defaultoptions "Direct link to defaultOptions()")

> **defaultOptions**(): `TSeriesOptions`

Default options

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`TSeriesOptions`

* * *

### destroy()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#destroy "Direct link to destroy()?")

> `optional` **destroy**(): `void`

This method will be evoked when the series has been removed from the chart. This method should be used to clean up any objects, references, and other items that could potentially cause memory leaks.

This method should contain all the necessary code to clean up the object before it is removed from memory. This includes removing any event listeners or timers that are attached to the object, removing any references to other objects, and resetting any values or properties that were modified during the lifetime of the object.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### conflationReducer()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#conflationreducer "Direct link to conflationReducer()?")

> `optional` **conflationReducer**(`item1`, `item2`): `TData`

Optional reducer used for conflation of custom data points. Given exactly 2 custom data contexts, should return a single aggregated item. Each context provides access to the original data plus metadata needed for conflation.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **item1**: [`CustomConflationContext`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext)
<`HorzScaleItem`, `TData`\>

• **item2**: [`CustomConflationContext`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomConflationContext)
<`HorzScaleItem`, `TData`\>

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`TData`

Defines where in the visual layer stack the renderer should be executed. Default is `'normal'`.

This method returns a renderer - special object to draw data

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **TSeriesAttachedParameters** = `unknown`

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#methods "Direct link to Methods")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### updateAllViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#updateallviews "Direct link to updateAllViews()?")

> `optional` **updateAllViews**(): `void`

This method is called when viewport has been changed, so primitive have to recalculate / invalidate its data

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### priceAxisViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#priceaxisviews "Direct link to priceAxisViews()?")

> `optional` **priceAxisViews**(): readonly [`ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView)
> \[\]

Returns array of labels to be drawn on the price axis used by the series

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

readonly [`ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView)
\[\]

array of objects; each of then must implement ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView interface

For performance reasons, the lightweight library uses internal caches based on references to arrays So, this method must return new array if set of views has changed and should try to return the same array if nothing changed

* * *

### timeAxisViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#timeaxisviews "Direct link to timeAxisViews()?")

> \[\]

readonly [`ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView)

array of objects; each of then must implement ISeriesPrimitiveAxisView interface

array of objects; each of then must implement ISeriesPrimitivePaneView interface

array of objects; each of then must implement ISeriesPrimitivePaneView interface

### timeAxisPaneViews()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#timeaxispaneviews "Direct link to timeAxisPaneViews()?")

> `optional` **timeAxisPaneViews**(): readonly [`IPrimitivePaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView)
> \[\]

\[\]

array of objects; each of then must implement ISeriesPrimitivePaneView interface

For performance reasons, the lightweight library uses internal caches based on references to arrays So, this method must return new array if set of views has changed and should try to return the same array if nothing changed

* * *

### autoscaleInfo()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#autoscaleinfo "Direct link to autoscaleInfo()?")

> `optional` **autoscaleInfo**(`startTimePoint`, `endTimePoint`): [`AutoscaleInfo`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AutoscaleInfo)

Return autoscaleInfo which will be merged with the series base autoscaleInfo. You can use this to expand the autoscale range to include visual elements drawn outside of the series' current visible price range.

**Important**: Please note that this method will be evoked very often during scrolling and zooming of the chart, thus it is recommended that this method is either simple to execute, or makes use of optimisations such as caching to ensure that the chart remains responsive.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **startTimePoint**: [`Logical`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Logical)

start time point for the current visible range

• **endTimePoint**: [`Logical`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Logical)

end time point for the current visible range

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

[`AutoscaleInfo`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AutoscaleInfo)

AutoscaleInfo

* * *

### attached()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#attached "Direct link to attached()?")

> `optional` **attached**(`param`): `void`

Attached Lifecycle hook.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **param**: `TSeriesAttachedParameters`

An object containing useful references for the attached primitive to use.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns-7 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

void

* * *

### detached()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#detached "Direct link to detached()?")

> `optional` **detached**(): `void`

Detached Lifecycle hook.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns-8 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

void

* * *

### hitTest()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#hittest "Direct link to hitTest()?")

> `optional` **hitTest**(`x`, `y`): [`PrimitiveHoveredItem`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem)

Hit test method which will be called by the library when the cursor is moved. Use this to register object ids being hovered for use within the crosshairMoved and click events emitted by the chart. Additionally, the hit test result can specify a preferred cursor type to display for the main chart pane. This method should return the top most hit for this primitive if more than one object is being intersected.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **x**: `number`

x Coordinate of mouse event

• **y**: `number`

y Coordinate of mouse event

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

[`PrimitiveHoveredItem`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem)

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#type-parameters)
    
*   [Methods](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveBase#methods)

# Interface: IPrimitivePaneView | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

This interface represents the primitive for one of the pane of the chart (main chart area, time scale, price scale).

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### zOrder()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#zorder "Direct link to zOrder()?")

> `optional` **zOrder**(): [`PrimitivePaneViewZOrder`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PrimitivePaneViewZOrder)

Defines where in the visual layer stack the renderer should be executed. Default is `'normal'`.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#returns "Direct link to Returns")

[`PrimitivePaneViewZOrder`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PrimitivePaneViewZOrder)

the desired position in the visual layer stack.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#see "Direct link to See")

[PrimitivePaneViewZOrder](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PrimitivePaneViewZOrder)

* * *

### renderer()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#renderer "Direct link to renderer()")

> **renderer**(): [`IPrimitivePaneRenderer`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneRenderer)

This method returns a renderer - special object to draw data

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IPrimitivePaneRenderer`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneRenderer)

an renderer object to be used for drawing, or `null` if we have nothing to draw.

*   [Methods](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPrimitivePaneView#methods)
