# Interface: PaneRendererCustomData\<HorzScaleItem, TData\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Data provide to the custom series pane view which can be used within the renderer for drawing the series data.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

• **TData** _extends_ [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bars[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#bars "Direct link to bars")

> **bars**: readonly [`CustomBarItemData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomBarItemData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`, `TData`\>\[\]

List of all the series' items and their x coordinates.

* * *

### barSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#barspacing "Direct link to barSpacing")

> **barSpacing**: `number`

Spacing between consecutive bars.

* * *

### visibleRange[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#visiblerange "Direct link to visibleRange")

> **visibleRange**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
> <`number`\>

The current visible range of items on the chart.

* * *

### conflationFactor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#conflationfactor "Direct link to conflationFactor")

> **conflationFactor**: `number`

Current conflation factor. The value represents how many data points have been combined to form this conflated data point. This can be used to calculate the effective bar spacing until the next data point. `effectiveBarSpacing = conflationFactor * barSpacing`. If you are rendering a non-continuous series (like a Candlestick instead of Line) then you likely would want to use the effectiveBarSpacing value for your width calculations.

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#type-parameters)
    
*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#properties)
    *   [bars](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#bars)
        
    *   [barSpacing](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#barspacing)
        
    *   [visibleRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#visiblerange)
        
    *   [conflationFactor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneRendererCustomData#conflationfactor)

---

# Interface: ImageWatermarkOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### maxWidth?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#maxwidth "Direct link to maxWidth?")

> `optional` **maxWidth**: `number`

Maximum width for the image watermark.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

    undefined

* * *

### maxHeight?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#maxheight "Direct link to maxHeight?")

> `optional` **maxHeight**: `number`

Maximum height for the image watermark.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

    undefined

* * *

### padding[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#padding "Direct link to padding")

> **padding**: `number`

Padding to maintain around the image watermark relative to the chart pane edges.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

    0

* * *

### alpha[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#alpha "Direct link to alpha")

> **alpha**: `number`

The alpha (opacity) for the image watermark. Where `1` is fully opaque (visible) and `0` is fully transparent.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

    1

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ImageWatermarkOptions#properties)

### formatTickmark()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#formattickmark "Direct link to formatTickmark()")

> **formatTickmark**(`item`, `localizationOptions`): `string`

Format the horizontal scale tickmark into a display string.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#parameters-8 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **item**: [`TickMark`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TickMark)

tickmark item

<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Localization options

`string`

string

* * *

### maxTickMarkWeight()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#maxtickmarkweight "Direct link to maxTickMarkWeight()")

> **maxTickMarkWeight**(`marks`): [`TickMarkWeightValue`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkWeightValue)

Returns the maximum tickmark weight value for the specified tickmarks on the time scale.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#parameters-9 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **marks**: [`TimeMark`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeMark)
\[\]

Timescale tick marks

[`TickMarkWeightValue`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkWeightValue)

TickMarkWeightValue

* * *

### fillWeightsForPoints()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#fillweightsforpoints "Direct link to fillWeightsForPoints()")

> **fillWeightsForPoints**(`sortedTimePoints`, `startIndex`): `void`

Fill the weights for the sorted time scale points.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#parameters-10 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **sortedTimePoints**: readonly [`Mutable`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Mutable)
 <[`TimeScalePoint`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScalePoint)
\>\[\]

starting index

`void`

* * *

### shouldResetTickmarkLabels()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#shouldresettickmarklabels "Direct link to shouldResetTickmarkLabels()?")

> `optional` **shouldResetTickmarkLabels**(`tickMarks`): `boolean`

If returns true, then the tick mark formatter will be called for all the visible tick marks even if the formatter has previously been called for a specific tick mark. This allows you to change the formatting on all the tick marks.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#parameters-11 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **tickMarks**: readonly [`TickMark`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TickMark)
\[\]

array of tick marks

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior#returns-12 "Direct link to Returns")

`boolean`

boolean

# Interface: PriceFormatBuiltIn | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents series value formatting options. The precision and minMove properties allow wide customization of formatting.

Examples[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#examples "Direct link to Examples")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    `minMove=0.01`, `precision` is not specified - prices will change like 1.13, 1.14, 1.15 etc.

    `minMove=0.01`, `precision=3` - prices will change like 1.130, 1.140, 1.150 etc.

    `minMove=0.05`, `precision` is not specified - prices will change like 1.10, 1.15, 1.20 etc.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### type[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#type "Direct link to type")

> **type**: `"percent"` | `"price"` | `"volume"`

Built-in price formats:

*   `'price'` is the most common choice; it allows customization of precision and rounding of prices.
*   `'volume'` uses abbreviation for formatting prices like `1.2K` or `12.67M`.
*   `'percent'` uses `%` sign at the end of prices.

* * *

### precision[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#precision "Direct link to precision")

> **precision**: `number`

Number of digits after the decimal point. If it is not set, then its value is calculated automatically based on minMove.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`2` if both [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove)
 and [precision](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#precision)
 are not provided, calculated automatically based on [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove)
 otherwise.

* * *

### minMove[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove "Direct link to minMove")

> **minMove**: `number`

The minimum possible step size for price value movement. This value shouldn't have more decimal digits than the precision.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`0.01`

* * *

### base?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#base "Direct link to base?")

> `optional` **base**: `number`

The base value for the price format. It should equal to 1 / [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove)
. If this option is specified, we ignore the [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove)
 option. It can be useful for cases with very small price movements like `1e-18` where we can reach limitations of floating point precision.

*   [Examples](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#examples)
    
*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#properties)
    *   [type](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#type)
        
    *   [precision](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#precision)
        
    *   [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#minmove)
        
    *   [base?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatBuiltIn#base)

---

# Interface: LayoutOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents layout options

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### background[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#background "Direct link to background")

> **background**: [`Background`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Background)

Chart and scales background color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`{ type: ColorType.Solid, color: '#FFFFFF' }`

* * *

### textColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#textcolor "Direct link to textColor")

> **textColor**: `string`

Color of text on the scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#191919'`

* * *

### fontSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#fontsize "Direct link to fontSize")

> **fontSize**: `number`

Font size of text on scales in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`12`

* * *

### fontFamily[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#fontfamily "Direct link to fontFamily")

> **fontFamily**: `string`

Font family of text on the scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Trebuchet MS', Roboto, Ubuntu, sans-serif`

* * *

### panes[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#panes "Direct link to panes")

> **panes**: [`LayoutPanesOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutPanesOptions)

Panes options.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`{ enableResize: true, separatorColor: '#2B2B43', separatorHoverColor: 'rgba(178, 181, 189, 0.2)'}`

* * *

### attributionLogo[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#attributionlogo "Direct link to attributionLogo")

> **attributionLogo**: `boolean`

Display the TradingView attribution logo on the main chart pane.

The licence for library specifies that you add the "attribution notice" from the NOTICE file to your code and a link to [https://www.tradingview.com/](https://www.tradingview.com/)
 to the page of your website or mobile application that is available to your users. Using this attribution logo is sufficient for meeting this linking requirement. However, if you already fulfill this requirement then you can disable this attribution logo.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

    true

* * *

### colorSpace[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#colorspace "Direct link to colorSpace")

> **colorSpace**: [`ColorSpace`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/ColorSpace)

Specifies the color space of the rendering context for the internal canvas elements.

Note: this option should only be specified during the chart creation and not changed at a later stage by using `applyOptions`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`srgb`

See [HTMLCanvasElement: getContext() method - Web APIs | MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLCanvasElement/getContext#colorspace)
 for more info

* * *

### colorParsers[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#colorparsers "Direct link to colorParsers")

> **colorParsers**: [`CustomColorParser`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomColorParser)
> \[\]

Array of custom color parser functions to handle color formats outside of standard sRGB values. Each parser function takes a string input and should return either:

*   An [Rgba](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Rgba)
     array \[r,g,b,a\] for valid colors (with values 0-255 for rgb and 0-1 for a)
*   null if the parser cannot handle that color string, allowing the next parser to attempt it

Parsers are tried in order until one returns a non-null result. This allows chaining multiple parsers to handle different color space formats.

Note: this option should only be specified during the chart creation and not changed at a later stage by using `applyOptions`.

The library already supports these color formats by default:

*   Hex colors (#RGB, #RGBA, #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA)
*   RGB/RGBA functions (rgb(), rgba())
*   HSL/HSLA functions (hsl(), hsla())
*   HWB function (hwb())
*   Named colors (red, blue, etc.)
*   'transparent' keyword

Custom parsers are only needed for other color spaces like:

*   Display P3: color(display-p3 r g b)
*   CIE Lab: lab(l a b)
*   LCH: lch(l c h)
*   Oklab: oklab(l a b)
*   Oklch: oklch(l c h)
*   ...

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#properties)
    *   [background](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#background)
        
    *   [textColor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#textcolor)
        
    *   [fontSize](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#fontsize)
        
    *   [fontFamily](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#fontfamily)
        
    *   [panes](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#panes)
        
    *   [attributionLogo](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#attributionlogo)
        
    *   [colorSpace](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#colorspace)
        
    *   [colorParsers](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#colorparsers)

---

> **lineType**: [`LineType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineType)

Line type.

    {@link LineType.Simple}

* * *

### lineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#linevisible "Direct link to lineVisible")

> **lineVisible**: `boolean`

Show series line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### pointMarkersVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#pointmarkersvisible "Direct link to pointMarkersVisible")

> **pointMarkersVisible**: `boolean`

Show circle markers on each point.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### pointMarkersRadius?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#pointmarkersradius "Direct link to pointMarkersRadius?")

> `optional` **pointMarkersRadius**: `number`

Circle markers radius in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkervisible "Direct link to crosshairMarkerVisible")

> **crosshairMarkerVisible**: `boolean`

Show the crosshair marker.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

### crosshairMarkerRadius[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerradius "Direct link to crosshairMarkerRadius")

### crosshairMarkerBorderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbordercolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderColor**: `string`

Crosshair marker border color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerbackgroundcolor "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor")

> **crosshairMarkerBackgroundColor**: `string`

The crosshair marker background color. An empty string falls back to the color of the series under the crosshair.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### crosshairMarkerBorderWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#crosshairmarkerborderwidth "Direct link to crosshairMarkerBorderWidth")

> **crosshairMarkerBorderWidth**: `number`

Crosshair marker border width in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`2`

* * *

### lastPriceAnimation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#lastpriceanimation "Direct link to lastPriceAnimation")

> **lastPriceAnimation**: [`LastPriceAnimationMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LastPriceAnimationMode)

Last price animation mode.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LastPriceAnimationMode.Disabled}

# Interface: LocalizationOptions\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents options for formatting dates, times, and prices according to a locale.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
    

Extended by[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#extended-by "Direct link to Extended by")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`PriceChartLocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions)
    

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### timeFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#timeformatter "Direct link to timeFormatter?")

> `optional` **timeFormatter**: [`TimeFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TimeFormatterFn)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Override formatting of the time scale crosshair label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### dateFormat[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#dateformat "Direct link to dateFormat")

> **dateFormat**: `string`

Date formatting string.

Can contain `yyyy`, `yy`, `MMMM`, `MMM`, `MM` and `dd` literals which will be replaced with corresponding date's value.

Ignored if [timeFormatter](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#timeformatter)
 has been specified.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'dd MMM \'yy'`

* * *

### locale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#locale "Direct link to locale")

> **locale**: `string`

Current locale used to format dates. Uses the browser's language settings by default.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#see "Direct link to See")

[https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global\_Objects/Intl#Locale\_identification\_and\_negotiation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl#Locale_identification_and_negotiation)

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`navigator.language`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
 . [`locale`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase#locale)

* * *

### priceFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#priceformatter "Direct link to priceFormatter?")

> `optional` **priceFormatter**: [`PriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceFormatterFn)

Override formatting of the price scale tick marks, labels and crosshair labels. Can be used for cases that can't be covered with built-in price formats.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#see-1 "Direct link to See")

[PriceFormatCustom](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom)

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
 . [`priceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase#priceformatter)

* * *

### tickmarksPriceFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#tickmarkspriceformatter "Direct link to tickmarksPriceFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickmarksPriceFormatter**: [`TickmarksPriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickmarksPriceFormatterFn)

Overrides the formatting of price scale tick marks. Use this to define formatting rules based on all provided price values.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
 . [`tickmarksPriceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase#tickmarkspriceformatter)

* * *

### percentageFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#percentageformatter "Direct link to percentageFormatter?")

> `optional` **percentageFormatter**: [`PercentageFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PercentageFormatterFn)

Overrides the formatting of percentage scale tick marks.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
 . [`percentageFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase#percentageformatter)

* * *

### tickmarksPercentageFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#tickmarkspercentageformatter "Direct link to tickmarksPercentageFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickmarksPercentageFormatter**: [`TickmarksPercentageFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickmarksPercentageFormatterFn)

Override formatting of the percentage scale tick marks. Can be used if formatting should be adjusted based on all the values being formatted

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptionsBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase)
 . [`tickmarksPercentageFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptionsBase#tickmarkspercentageformatter)

# Interface: PriceFormatCustom | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents series value formatting options.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### type[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#type "Direct link to type")

> **type**: `"custom"`

The custom price format.

* * *

### formatter[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#formatter "Direct link to formatter")

> **formatter**: [`PriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceFormatterFn)

Override price formatting behaviour. Can be used for cases that can't be covered with built-in price formats.

* * *

### tickmarksFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#tickmarksformatter "Direct link to tickmarksFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickmarksFormatter**: [`TickmarksPriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickmarksPriceFormatterFn)

Override price formatting for multiple prices. Can be used if formatter should be adjusted based of all values being formatted.

* * *

### minMove[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#minmove "Direct link to minMove")

> **minMove**: `number`

The minimum possible step size for price value movement.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`0.01`

* * *

### base?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#base "Direct link to base?")

> `optional` **base**: `number`

The base value for the price format. It should equal to 1 / [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#minmove)
. If this option is specified, we ignore the [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#minmove)
 option. It can be useful for cases with very small price movements like `1e-18` where we can reach limitations of floating point precision.

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#properties)
    *   [type](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#type)
        
    *   [formatter](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#formatter)
        
    *   [tickmarksFormatter?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#tickmarksformatter)
        
    *   [minMove](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#minmove)
        
    *   [base?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom#base)

---

# Interface: SeriesDataItemTypeMap\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the type of data that a series contains.

For example a bar series contains [BarData](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarData)
 or [WhitespaceData](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bar[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap#bar "Direct link to Bar")

> **Bar**: [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\> | [`BarData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

The types of bar series data.

* * *

### Candlestick[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap#candlestick "Direct link to Candlestick")

# Interface: PriceScaleOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Structure that describes price scale options

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autoScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#autoscale "Direct link to autoScale")

> **autoScale**: `boolean`

Autoscaling is a feature that automatically adjusts a price scale to fit the visible range of data. Note that overlay price scales are always auto-scaled.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### mode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#mode "Direct link to mode")

> **mode**: [`PriceScaleMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceScaleMode)

Price scale mode.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link PriceScaleMode.Normal}

* * *

### invertScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#invertscale "Direct link to invertScale")

> **invertScale**: `boolean`

Invert the price scale, so that a upwards trend is shown as a downwards trend and vice versa. Affects both the price scale and the data on the chart.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### alignLabels[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#alignlabels "Direct link to alignLabels")

> **alignLabels**: `boolean`

Align price scale labels to prevent them from overlapping.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### scaleMargins[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#scalemargins "Direct link to scaleMargins")

> **scaleMargins**: [`PriceScaleMargins`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleMargins)

Price scale margins.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`{ bottom: 0.1, top: 0.2 }`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#example "Direct link to Example")

    chart.priceScale('right').applyOptions({    scaleMargins: {        top: 0.8,        bottom: 0,    },});

* * *

### borderVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#bordervisible "Direct link to borderVisible")

> **borderVisible**: `boolean`

Set true to draw a border between the price scale and the chart area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### borderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#bordercolor "Direct link to borderColor")

> **borderColor**: `string`

Price scale border color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#2B2B43'`

* * *

### textColor?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#textcolor "Direct link to textColor?")

> `optional` **textColor**: `string`

Price scale text color. If not provided [LayoutOptions.textColor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LayoutOptions#textcolor)
 is used.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

* * *

### entireTextOnly[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#entiretextonly "Direct link to entireTextOnly")

> **entireTextOnly**: `boolean`

Show top and bottom corner labels only if entire text is visible.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Indicates if this price scale visible. Ignored by overlay price scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true` for the right price scale and `false` for the left. For the yield curve chart, the default is for the left scale to be visible.

* * *

### ticksVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#ticksvisible "Direct link to ticksVisible")

> **ticksVisible**: `boolean`

Draw small horizontal line on price axis labels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

* * *

### minimumWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#minimumwidth "Direct link to minimumWidth")

> **minimumWidth**: `number`

Define a minimum width for the price scale. Note: This value will be exceeded if the price scale needs more space to display it's contents.

Setting a minimum width could be useful for ensuring that multiple charts positioned in a vertical stack each have an identical price scale width, or for plugins which require a bit more space within the price scale pane.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

    0

* * *

### ensureEdgeTickMarksVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#ensureedgetickmarksvisible "Direct link to ensureEdgeTickMarksVisible")

> **ensureEdgeTickMarksVisible**: `boolean`

Ensures that tick marks are always visible at the very top and bottom of the price scale, regardless of the data range. When enabled, a tick mark will be drawn at both edges of the scale, providing clear boundary indicators.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#properties)
    *   [autoScale](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#autoscale)
        
    *   [mode](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#mode)
        
    *   [invertScale](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#invertscale)
        
    *   [alignLabels](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#alignlabels)
        
    *   [scaleMargins](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#scalemargins)
        
    *   [borderVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#bordervisible)
        
    *   [borderColor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#bordercolor)
        
    *   [textColor?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#textcolor)
        
    *   [entireTextOnly](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#entiretextonly)
        
    *   [visible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#visible)
        
    *   [ticksVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#ticksvisible)
        
    *   [minimumWidth](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#minimumwidth)
        
    *   [ensureEdgeTickMarksVisible](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceScaleOptions#ensureedgetickmarksvisible)

---

# Interface: PriceLineOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents a price line options.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### id?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#id "Direct link to id?")

> `optional` **id**: `string`

The optional ID of this price line.

* * *

### price[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#price "Direct link to price")

> **price**: `number`

Price line's value.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`0`

* * *

### color[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#color "Direct link to color")

> **color**: `string`

Price line's color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### lineWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#linewidth "Direct link to lineWidth")

> **lineWidth**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Price line's width in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`1`

* * *

### lineStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#linestyle "Direct link to lineStyle")

> **lineStyle**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Price line's style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.Solid}

* * *

### lineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#linevisible "Direct link to lineVisible")

> **lineVisible**: `boolean`

Display line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### axisLabelVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#axislabelvisible "Direct link to axisLabelVisible")

> **axisLabelVisible**: `boolean`

Display the current price value in on the price scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### title[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#title "Direct link to title")

> **title**: `string`

Price line's on the chart pane.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### axisLabelColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#axislabelcolor "Direct link to axisLabelColor")

> **axisLabelColor**: `string`

Background color for the axis label. Will default to the price line color if unspecified.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### axisLabelTextColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#axislabeltextcolor "Direct link to axisLabelTextColor")

> **axisLabelTextColor**: `string`

Text color for the axis label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceLineOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

# Interface: IChartApi | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

The main interface of a single chart using time for horizontal scale.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#extends "Direct link to Extends")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
     <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
    \>

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### applyOptions()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#applyoptions "Direct link to applyOptions()")

> **applyOptions**(`options`): `void`

Applies new options to the chart

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **options**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
 <[`TimeChartOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions)
\>

Any subset of options.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Overrides[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#overrides "Direct link to Overrides")

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
 . [`applyOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#applyoptions)

* * *

### remove()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#remove "Direct link to remove()")

> **remove**(): `void`

Removes the chart object including all DOM elements. This is an irreversible operation, you cannot do anything with the chart after removing it.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
 . [`remove`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#remove)

* * *

### resize()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#resize "Direct link to resize()")

> **resize**(`width`, `height`, `forceRepaint`?): `void`

Sets fixed size of the chart. By default chart takes up 100% of its container.

If chart has the `autoSize` option enabled, and the ResizeObserver is available then the width and height values will be ignored.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **width**: `number`

Target width of the chart.

• **height**: `number`

Target height of the chart.

• **forceRepaint?**: `boolean`

True to initiate resize immediately. One could need this to get screenshot immediately after resize.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
 . [`resize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#resize)

* * *

### addCustomSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#addcustomseries "Direct link to addCustomSeries()")

> **addCustomSeries**<`TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>(`customPaneView`, `customOptions`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`"Custom"`, [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
> , `TData` | [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
>  <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
> \>, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

Creates a custom series with specified parameters.

A custom series is a generic series which can be extended with a custom renderer to implement chart types which the library doesn't support by default.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **TData** _extends_ [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
 <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\>

• **TOptions** _extends_ [`CustomSeriesOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesOptions)

• **TPartialOptions** _extends_ [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\> = [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **customPaneView**: [`ICustomSeriesPaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView)
 <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
, `TData`, `TOptions`\>

A custom series pane view which implements the custom renderer.

• **customOptions?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

### addSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#addseries "Direct link to addSeries()")

> **addSeries**<`T`\>(`definition`, `options`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`T`, [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
> , [`SeriesDataItemTypeMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap)
>  <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
> \>\[`T`\], [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\]>

Creates a series with specified parameters.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **T** _extends_ keyof [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

<`T`\>

A series definition.

• **options?**: [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
\[`T`\]

Customization parameters of the series being created.

• **paneIndex?**: `number`

An index of the pane where the series should be created.

    const series = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, { lineWidth: 2 });

, [`SeriesDataItemTypeMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap)
 <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\>\[`T`\], [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
\[`T`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)

### removeSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#removeseries "Direct link to removeSeries()")

> **removeSeries**(`seriesApi`): `void`

### subscribeClick()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#subscribeclick "Direct link to subscribeClick()")

> **subscribeClick**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the chart click event.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`MouseEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/MouseEventHandler)
 <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
\>

Handler to be called on mouse click.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#inherited-from-5 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
 . [`subscribeClick`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#subscribeclick)

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    function myClickHandler(param) {    if (!param.point) {        return;    }    console.log(`Click at ${param.point.x}, ${param.point.y}. The time is ${param.time}.`);}chart.subscribeClick(myClickHandler);

* * *

### takeScreenshot()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#takescreenshot "Direct link to takeScreenshot()")

> **takeScreenshot**(`addTopLayer`?, `includeCrosshair`?): `HTMLCanvasElement`

Make a screenshot of the chart with all the elements excluding crosshair.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-12 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **addTopLayer?**: `boolean`

if true, the top layer and primitives will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

• **includeCrosshair?**: `boolean`

works only if addTopLayer is enabled. If true, the crosshair will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#returns-15 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLCanvasElement`

A canvas with the chart drawn on. Any `Canvas` methods like `toDataURL()` or `toBlob()` can be used to serialize the result.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
 . [`takeScreenshot`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#takescreenshot)

### setCrosshairPosition()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#setcrosshairposition "Direct link to setCrosshairPosition()")

> **setCrosshairPosition**(`price`, `horizontalPosition`, `seriesApi`): `void`

Set the crosshair position within the chart.

Usually the crosshair position is set automatically by the user's actions. However in some cases you may want to set it explicitly.

For example if you want to synchronise the crosshairs of two separate charts.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **price**: `number`

The price (vertical coordinate) of the new crosshair position.

• **horizontalPosition**: [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

The horizontal coordinate (time by default) of the new crosshair position.

• **seriesApi**: [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)

# Interface: PriceChartOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Configuration options specific to price-based charts. Extends the base chart options and includes localization settings for price formatting.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
    <`number`\>

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: `number`

Width of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`width`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#width)

* * *

### height[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#height "Direct link to height")

> **height**: `number`

Height of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`height`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#height)

* * *

### autoSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#autosize "Direct link to autoSize")

> **autoSize**: `boolean`

Setting this flag to `true` will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes.

This feature requires [`ResizeObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver)
 class to be available in the global scope. Note that calling code is responsible for providing a polyfill if required. If the global scope does not have `ResizeObserver`, a warning will appear and the flag will be ignored.

Please pay attention that `autoSize` option and explicit sizes options `width` and `height` don't conflict with one another. If you specify `autoSize` flag, then `width` and `height` options will be ignored unless `ResizeObserver` has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag `autoSize` could also be set with and unset with `applyOptions` function.

    const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, {    autoSize: true,});

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`autoSize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#autosize)

* * *

### trackingMode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#trackingmode "Direct link to trackingMode")

> **trackingMode**: [`TrackingModeOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions)

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`trackingMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#trackingmode)

* * *

### addDefaultPane[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#adddefaultpane "Direct link to addDefaultPane")

> **addDefaultPane**: `boolean`

Whether to add a default pane to the chart Disable this option when you want to create a chart with no panes and add them manually

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

 . [`addDefaultPane`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#adddefaultpane)

# Interface: PrimitiveHoveredItem | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Data representing the currently hovered object from the Hit test.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### cursorStyle?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#cursorstyle "Direct link to cursorStyle?")

> `optional` **cursorStyle**: `string`

CSS cursor style as defined here: [MDN: CSS Cursor](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/cursor)
 or `undefined` if you want the library to use the default cursor style instead.

* * *

### externalId[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#externalid "Direct link to externalId")

> **externalId**: `string`

Hovered objects external ID. Can be used to identify the source item within a mouse subscriber event.

* * *

### zOrder[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#zorder "Direct link to zOrder")

> **zOrder**: [`PrimitivePaneViewZOrder`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PrimitivePaneViewZOrder)

The zOrder of the hovered item.

* * *

### isBackground?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#isbackground "Direct link to isBackground?")

> `optional` **isBackground**: `boolean`

Set to true if the object is rendered using `drawBackground` instead of `draw`.

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#properties)
    *   [cursorStyle?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#cursorstyle)
        
    *   [externalId](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#externalid)
        
    *   [zOrder](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#zorder)
        
    *   [isBackground?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PrimitiveHoveredItem#isbackground)

# Interface: SeriesPartialOptionsMap | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the type of partial options for each series type.

For example a bar series has options represented by [BarSeriesPartialOptions](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/BarSeriesPartialOptions)
.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bar[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#bar "Direct link to Bar")

> **Bar**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`BarStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \>

The type of bar series partial options.

* * *

### Candlestick[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#candlestick "Direct link to Candlestick")

> **Candlestick**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`CandlestickStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \>

The type of candlestick series partial options.

* * *

### Area[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#area "Direct link to Area")

> **Area**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`AreaStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \>

The type of area series partial options.

* * *

### Baseline[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#baseline "Direct link to Baseline")

> **Baseline**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`BaselineStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \>

The type of baseline series partial options.

* * *

### Line[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#line "Direct link to Line")

> **Line**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`LineStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \>

The type of line series partial options.

* * *

### Histogram[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap#histogram "Direct link to Histogram")

> **Histogram**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`HistogramStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HistogramStyleOptions)
