# Interface: IPaneApi\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the interface for interacting with a pane in a lightweight chart.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### getHeight()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#getheight "Direct link to getHeight()")

> **getHeight**(): `number`

Retrieves the height of the pane in pixels.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

The height of the pane in pixels.

* * *

### setHeight()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#setheight "Direct link to setHeight()")

> **setHeight**(`height`): `void`

Sets the height of the pane.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **height**: `number`

The number of pixels to set as the height of the pane.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### moveTo()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#moveto "Direct link to moveTo()")

> **moveTo**(`paneIndex`): `void`

Moves the pane to a new position.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **paneIndex**: `number`

The target index of the pane. Should be a number between 0 and the total number of panes - 1.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### paneIndex()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#paneindex "Direct link to paneIndex()")

> **paneIndex**(): `number`

Retrieves the index of the pane.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

The index of the pane. It is a number between 0 and the total number of panes - 1.

* * *

### getSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#getseries "Direct link to getSeries()")

> **getSeries**(): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <keyof [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
> , `HorzScaleItem`, [`AreaData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\> | [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)

### getStretchFactor()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#getstretchfactor "Direct link to getStretchFactor()")

> **getStretchFactor**(): `number`

Returns the stretch factor of the pane. Stretch factor determines the relative size of the pane compared to other panes.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-11 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

The stretch factor of the pane. Default is 1

* * *

### setStretchFactor()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#setstretchfactor "Direct link to setStretchFactor()")

> **setStretchFactor**(`stretchFactor`): `void`

Sets the stretch factor of the pane. When you creating a pane, the stretch factor is 1 by default. So if you have three panes, and you want to make the first pane twice as big as the second and third panes, you can set the stretch factor of the first pane to 2000. Example:

    const pane1 = chart.addPane();const pane2 = chart.addPane();const pane3 = chart.addPane();pane1.setStretchFactor(0.2);pane2.setStretchFactor(0.3);pane3.setStretchFactor(0.5);// Now the first pane will be 20% of the total height, the second pane will be 30% of the total height, and the third pane will be 50% of the total height.// Note: if you have one pane with default stretch factor of 1 and set other pane's stretch factor to 50,// library will try to make second pane 50 times smaller than the first pane

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **stretchFactor**: `number`

The stretch factor of the pane.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-12 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### addCustomSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#addcustomseries "Direct link to addCustomSeries()")

> **addCustomSeries**<`TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>(`customPaneView`, `customOptions`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`"Custom"`, `HorzScaleItem`, [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\> | `TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

Creates a custom series with specified parameters.

A custom series is a generic series which can be extended with a custom renderer to implement chart types which the library doesn't support by default.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **TData** _extends_ [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

• **TOptions** _extends_ [`CustomSeriesOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesOptions)

• **TPartialOptions** _extends_ [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\> = [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#parameters-7 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **customPaneView**: [`ICustomSeriesPaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView)
<`HorzScaleItem`, `TData`, `TOptions`\>

A custom series pane view which implements the custom renderer.

• **customOptions?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

Customization parameters of the series being created.

    const series = pane.addCustomSeries(myCustomPaneView);

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi#returns-13 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
<`"Custom"`, `HorzScaleItem`, [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\> | `TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

* * *

# Interface: IChartApiBase\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

The main interface of a single chart.

Extended by[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#extended-by "Direct link to Extended by")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
    

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#methods "Direct link to Methods")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### remove()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#remove "Direct link to remove()")

> **remove**(): `void`

Removes the chart object including all DOM elements. This is an irreversible operation, you cannot do anything with the chart after removing it.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### resize()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#resize "Direct link to resize()")

> **resize**(`width`, `height`, `forceRepaint`?): `void`

Sets fixed size of the chart. By default chart takes up 100% of its container.

If chart has the `autoSize` option enabled, and the ResizeObserver is available then the width and height values will be ignored.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **width**: `number`

Target width of the chart.

• **height**: `number`

Target height of the chart.

• **forceRepaint?**: `boolean`

True to initiate resize immediately. One could need this to get screenshot immediately after resize.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### addCustomSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#addcustomseries "Direct link to addCustomSeries()")

> **addCustomSeries**<`TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>(`customPaneView`, `customOptions`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`"Custom"`, `HorzScaleItem`, `TData` | [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

Creates a custom series with specified parameters.

A custom series is a generic series which can be extended with a custom renderer to implement chart types which the library doesn't support by default.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **TData** _extends_ [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

• **TOptions** _extends_ [`CustomSeriesOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesOptions)

• **TPartialOptions** _extends_ [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\> = [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **customPaneView**: [`ICustomSeriesPaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView)
<`HorzScaleItem`, `TData`, `TOptions`\>

A custom series pane view which implements the custom renderer.

• **customOptions?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

Customization parameters of the series being created.

    const series = chart.addCustomSeries(myCustomPaneView);

• **paneIndex?**: `number`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
<`"Custom"`, `HorzScaleItem`, `TData` | [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

### addSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#addseries "Direct link to addSeries()")

> **addSeries**<`T`\>(`definition`, `options`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`T`, `HorzScaleItem`, [`SeriesDataItemTypeMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>\[`T`\], [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\]>

Creates a series with specified parameters.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#type-parameters-2 "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **T** _extends_ keyof [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **definition**: [`SeriesDefinition`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDefinition)
<`T`\>

A series definition.

• **options?**: [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
\[`T`\]

Customization parameters of the series being created.

• **paneIndex?**: `number`

An index of the pane where the series should be created.

    const series = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, { lineWidth: 2 });

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)

\[`T`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
\[`T`\]>

### subscribeClick()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#subscribeclick "Direct link to subscribeClick()")

> **subscribeClick**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the chart click event.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-4 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`MouseEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/MouseEventHandler)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Handler to be called on mouse click.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    function myClickHandler(param) {    if (!param.point) {        return;    }    console.log(`Click at ${param.point.x}, ${param.point.y}. The time is ${param.time}.`);}chart.subscribeClick(myClickHandler);

* * *

### unsubscribeClick()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#unsubscribeclick "Direct link to unsubscribeClick()")

> **unsubscribeClick**(`handler`): `void`

Unsubscribe a handler that was previously subscribed using [subscribeClick](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#subscribeclick)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`MouseEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/MouseEventHandler)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Previously subscribed handler

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#example-2 "Direct link to Example")

    chart.unsubscribeClick(myClickHandler);

* * *

### takeScreenshot()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#takescreenshot "Direct link to takeScreenshot()")

> **takeScreenshot**(`addTopLayer`?, `includeCrosshair`?): `HTMLCanvasElement`

Make a screenshot of the chart with all the elements excluding crosshair.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-12 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **addTopLayer?**: `boolean`

if true, the top layer and primitives will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

• **includeCrosshair?**: `boolean`

works only if addTopLayer is enabled. If true, the crosshair will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-15 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLCanvasElement`

A canvas with the chart drawn on. Any `Canvas` methods like `toDataURL()` or `toBlob()` can be used to serialize the result.

* * *

> **autoSizeActive**(): `boolean`

`boolean`

### chartElement()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#chartelement "Direct link to chartElement()")

> **chartElement**(): `HTMLDivElement`

Returns the generated div element containing the chart. This can be used for adding your own additional event listeners, or for measuring the elements dimensions and position within the document.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#returns-21 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLDivElement`

generated div element containing the chart.

* * *

### setCrosshairPosition()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#setcrosshairposition "Direct link to setCrosshairPosition()")

> **setCrosshairPosition**(`price`, `horizontalPosition`, `seriesApi`): `void`

Set the crosshair position within the chart.

Usually the crosshair position is set automatically by the user's actions. However in some cases you may want to set it explicitly.

For example if you want to synchronise the crosshairs of two separate charts.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **price**: `number`

The price (vertical coordinate) of the new crosshair position.

• **horizontalPosition**: `HorzScaleItem`

The horizontal coordinate (time by default) of the new crosshair position.

• **seriesApi**: [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
<keyof [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
, `HorzScaleItem`, [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\> | [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
<`HorzScaleItem`\> | [`AreaData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaData)

### paneSize()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase#panesize "Direct link to paneSize()")

> **paneSize**(`paneIndex`?): [`PaneSize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PaneSize)

Dimensions of the chart pane

# Interface: ITimeScaleApi\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Interface to chart time scale

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem**

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### scrollPosition()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#scrollposition "Direct link to scrollPosition()")

> **scrollPosition**(): `number`

Return the distance from the right edge of the time scale to the lastest bar of the series measured in bars.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

* * *

### scrollToPosition()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#scrolltoposition "Direct link to scrollToPosition()")

> **scrollToPosition**(`position`, `animated`): `void`

Scrolls the chart to the specified position.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **position**: `number`

Target data position

• **animated**: `boolean`

Setting this to true makes the chart scrolling smooth and adds animation

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### scrollToRealTime()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#scrolltorealtime "Direct link to scrollToRealTime()")

> **scrollToRealTime**(): `void`

Restores default scroll position of the chart. This process is always animated.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getVisibleRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#getvisiblerange "Direct link to getVisibleRange()")

> **getVisibleRange**(): [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Returns current visible time range of the chart.

Note that this method cannot extrapolate time and will use the only currently existent data. To get complete information about current visible range, please use [getVisibleLogicalRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#getvisiblelogicalrange)
 and [ISeriesApi.barsInLogicalRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#barsinlogicalrange)
.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Visible range or null if the chart has no data at all.

* * *

### setVisibleRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#setvisiblerange "Direct link to setVisibleRange()")

> **setVisibleRange**(`range`): `void`

Sets visible range of data.

Note that this method cannot extrapolate time and will use the only currently existent data. Thus, for example, if currently a chart doesn't have data prior `2018-01-01` date and you set visible range with `from` date `2016-01-01`, it will be automatically adjusted to `2018-01-01` (and the same for `to` date).

But if you can approximate indexes on your own - you could use [setVisibleLogicalRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#setvisiblelogicalrange)
 instead.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **range**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Target visible range of data.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#example "Direct link to Example")

    chart.timeScale().setVisibleRange({    from: (new Date(Date.UTC(2018, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0))).getTime() / 1000,    to: (new Date(Date.UTC(2018, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0))).getTime() / 1000,});

* * *

### getVisibleLogicalRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#getvisiblelogicalrange "Direct link to getVisibleLogicalRange()")

> **getVisibleLogicalRange**(): [`LogicalRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LogicalRange)

Returns the current visible [logical range](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/time-scale#logical-range)
 of the chart as an object with the first and last time points of the logical range, or returns `null` if the chart has no data.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

[`LogicalRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LogicalRange)

Visible range or null if the chart has no data at all.

* * *

### setVisibleLogicalRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#setvisiblelogicalrange "Direct link to setVisibleLogicalRange()")

> **setVisibleLogicalRange**(`range`): `void`

Sets visible [logical range](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/time-scale#logical-range)
 of data.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **range**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`number`\>

Target visible logical range of data.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    chart.timeScale().setVisibleLogicalRange({ from: 0, to: 10 });

* * *

Converts a logical index to local x coordinate.

Logical index needs to be converted

[`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

x coordinate of that time or `null` if the chart doesn't have data

* * *

### coordinateToLogical()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#coordinatetological "Direct link to coordinateToLogical()")

Coordinate needs to be converted

[`Logical`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Logical)

Logical index that is located on that coordinate or `null` if the chart doesn't have data

[`TimePointIndex`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TimePointIndex)

Converts a time to local x coordinate.

[`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

X coordinate of that time or `null` if no time found on time scale

* * *

### subscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#subscribevisibletimerangechange "Direct link to subscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange()")

> **subscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the visible time range change events.

The argument passed to the handler function is an object with `from` and `to` properties of type [Time](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
, or `null` if there is no visible data.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters-8 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`TimeRangeChangeEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TimeRangeChangeEventHandler)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Handler (function) to be called when the visible indexes change.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-16 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#example-2 "Direct link to Example")

    function myVisibleTimeRangeChangeHandler(newVisibleTimeRange) {    if (newVisibleTimeRange === null) {        // handle null    }    // handle new logical range}chart.timeScale().subscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange(myVisibleTimeRangeChangeHandler);

* * *

### unsubscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#unsubscribevisibletimerangechange "Direct link to unsubscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange()")

> **unsubscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange**(`handler`): `void`

Unsubscribe a handler that was previously subscribed using [subscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#subscribevisibletimerangechange)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters-9 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`TimeRangeChangeEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TimeRangeChangeEventHandler)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Previously subscribed handler

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-17 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#example-3 "Direct link to Example")

    chart.timeScale().unsubscribeVisibleTimeRangeChange(myVisibleTimeRangeChangeHandler);

* * *

### subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#subscribevisiblelogicalrangechange "Direct link to subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange()")

> **subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the visible logical range change events.

The argument passed to the handler function is an object with `from` and `to` properties of type `number`, or `null` if there is no visible data.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#parameters-10 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`LogicalRangeChangeEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LogicalRangeChangeEventHandler)

Handler (function) to be called when the visible indexes change.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#returns-18 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ITimeScaleApi#example-4 "Direct link to Example")

    function myVisibleLogicalRangeChangeHandler(newVisibleLogicalRange) {    if (newVisibleLogicalRange === null) {        // handle null    }    // handle new logical range}chart.timeScale().subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange(myVisibleLogicalRangeChangeHandler);

* * *

# Interface: ISeriesApi\<TSeriesType, HorzScaleItem, TData, TOptions, TPartialOptions\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the interface for interacting with series.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **TSeriesType** _extends_ [`SeriesType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/SeriesType)

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

• **TData** = [`SeriesDataItemTypeMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>\[`TSeriesType`\]

• **TOptions** = [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
\[`TSeriesType`\]

• **TPartialOptions** = [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
\[`TSeriesType`\]

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### priceFormatter()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#priceformatter "Direct link to priceFormatter()")

> **priceFormatter**(): [`IPriceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceFormatter)

Returns current price formatter

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

[`IPriceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceFormatter)

Interface to the price formatter object that can be used to format prices in the same way as the chart does

* * *

### priceToCoordinate()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#pricetocoordinate "Direct link to priceToCoordinate()")

> **priceToCoordinate**(`price`): [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

Converts specified series price to pixel coordinate according to the series price scale

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **price**: `number`

Input price to be converted

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

[`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

Pixel coordinate of the price level on the chart

* * *

### coordinateToPrice()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#coordinatetoprice "Direct link to coordinateToPrice()")

> **coordinateToPrice**(`coordinate`): [`BarPrice`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/BarPrice)

Converts specified coordinate to price value according to the series price scale

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **coordinate**: `number`

Input coordinate to be converted

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

[`BarPrice`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/BarPrice)

Price value of the coordinate on the chart

* * *

### barsInLogicalRange()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#barsinlogicalrange "Direct link to barsInLogicalRange()")

> **barsInLogicalRange**(`range`): [`BarsInfo`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Returns bars information for the series in the provided [logical range](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/time-scale#logical-range)
 or `null`, if no series data has been found in the requested range. This method can be used, for instance, to implement downloading historical data while scrolling to prevent a user from seeing empty space.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **range**: [`IRange`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IRange)
<`number`\>

The [logical range](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/time-scale#logical-range)
 to retrieve info for.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

[`BarsInfo`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarsInfo)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

The bars info for the given logical range.

#### Examples[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#examples "Direct link to Examples")

    const barsInfo = series.barsInLogicalRange(chart.timeScale().getVisibleLogicalRange());console.log(barsInfo);

    function onVisibleLogicalRangeChanged(newVisibleLogicalRange) {    const barsInfo = series.barsInLogicalRange(newVisibleLogicalRange);    // if there less than 50 bars to the left of the visible area    if (barsInfo !== null && barsInfo.barsBefore < 50) {        // try to load additional historical data and prepend it to the series data    }}chart.timeScale().subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange(onVisibleLogicalRangeChanged);

* * *

### applyOptions()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#applyoptions "Direct link to applyOptions()")

> **applyOptions**(`options`): `void`

Applies new options to the existing series You can set options initially when you create series or use the `applyOptions` method of the series to change the existing options. Note that you can only pass options you want to change.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **options**: `TPartialOptions`

Any subset of options.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### options()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#options "Direct link to options()")

> **options**(): `Readonly`<`TOptions`\>

Returns currently applied options

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`Readonly`<`TOptions`\>

Full set of currently applied options, including defaults

* * *

### priceScale()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#pricescale "Direct link to priceScale()")

> **priceScale**(): [`IPriceScaleApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi)

Returns the API interface for controlling the price scale that this series is currently attached to.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IPriceScaleApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceScaleApi)

IPriceScaleApi An interface for controlling the price scale (axis component) currently used by this series

#### Remarks[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#remarks "Direct link to Remarks")

Important: The returned PriceScaleApi is bound to the specific price scale (by ID and pane) that the series is using at the time this method is called. If you later move the series to a different pane or attach it to a different price scale (e.g., from 'right' to 'left'), the previously returned PriceScaleApi will NOT follow the series. It will continue to control the original price scale it was created for.

To control the new price scale after moving a series, you must call this method again to get a fresh PriceScaleApi instance for the current price scale.

* * *

### setData()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata "Direct link to setData()")

> **setData**(`data`): `void`

Sets or replaces series data.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-4 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **data**: `TData`\[\]

Ordered (earlier time point goes first) array of data items. Old data is fully replaced with the new one.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-7 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Examples[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#examples-1 "Direct link to Examples")

    lineSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-12', value: 24.11 },    { time: '2018-12-13', value: 31.74 },]);

    barSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-19', open: 141.77, high: 170.39, low: 120.25, close: 145.72 },    { time: '2018-12-20', open: 145.72, high: 147.99, low: 100.11, close: 108.19 },]);

* * *

### update()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update "Direct link to update()")

> **update**(`bar`, `historicalUpdate`?): `void`

Adds new data item to the existing set (or updates the latest item if times of the passed/latest items are equal).

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **bar**: `TData`

A single data item to be added. Time of the new item must be greater or equal to the latest existing time point. If the new item's time is equal to the last existing item's time, then the existing item is replaced with the new one.

• **historicalUpdate?**: `boolean`

If true, allows updating an existing data point that is not the latest bar. Default is false. Updating older data using `historicalUpdate` will be slower than updating the most recent data point.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-8 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Examples[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#examples-2 "Direct link to Examples")

    lineSeries.update({    time: '2018-12-12',    value: 24.11,});

    barSeries.update({    time: '2018-12-19',    open: 141.77,    high: 170.39,    low: 120.25,    close: 145.72,});

* * *

### pop()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#pop "Direct link to pop()")

> **pop**(`count`): `TData`\[\]

Removes one or more data items from the end of the series.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **count**: `number`

The number of data items to remove.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

`TData`\[\]

The removed data items.

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example "Direct link to Example")

    const removedData = lineSeries.pop(1);console.log(removedData);

* * *

### dataByIndex()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#databyindex "Direct link to dataByIndex()")

> **dataByIndex**(`logicalIndex`, `mismatchDirection`?): `TData`

Returns a bar data by provided logical index.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-7 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **logicalIndex**: `number`

Logical index

• **mismatchDirection?**: [`MismatchDirection`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/MismatchDirection)

Search direction if no data found at provided logical index.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-10 "Direct link to Returns")

`TData`

Original data item provided via setData or update methods.

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    const originalData = series.dataByIndex(10, LightweightCharts.MismatchDirection.NearestLeft);

* * *

### data()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#data "Direct link to data()")

> **data**(): readonly `TData`\[\]

Returns all the bar data for the series.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-11 "Direct link to Returns")

readonly `TData`\[\]

Original data items provided via setData or update methods.

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-2 "Direct link to Example")

    const originalData = series.data();

* * *

### subscribeDataChanged()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#subscribedatachanged "Direct link to subscribeDataChanged()")

> **subscribeDataChanged**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the data changed event. This event is fired whenever the `update` or `setData` method is evoked on the series.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-8 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`DataChangedHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DataChangedHandler)

Handler to be called on a data changed event.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-12 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-3 "Direct link to Example")

    function myHandler() {    const data = series.data();    console.log(`The data has changed. New Data length: ${data.length}`);}series.subscribeDataChanged(myHandler);

* * *

### unsubscribeDataChanged()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#unsubscribedatachanged "Direct link to unsubscribeDataChanged()")

> **unsubscribeDataChanged**(`handler`): `void`

Unsubscribe a handler that was previously subscribed using [subscribeDataChanged](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#subscribedatachanged)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-9 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`DataChangedHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DataChangedHandler)

Previously subscribed handler

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-13 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-4 "Direct link to Example")

    chart.unsubscribeDataChanged(myHandler);

* * *

### createPriceLine()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#createpriceline "Direct link to createPriceLine()")

> **createPriceLine**(`options`): [`IPriceLine`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine)

Creates a new price line

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-10 "Direct link to Parameters")

Any subset of options, however `price` is required.

[`IPriceLine`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine)

### priceLines()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#pricelines "Direct link to priceLines()")

> **priceLines**(): [`IPriceLine`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine)
> \[\]

Returns an array of price lines.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-16 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IPriceLine`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPriceLine)
\[\]

* * *

### seriesType()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#seriestype "Direct link to seriesType()")

> **seriesType**(): `TSeriesType`

Return current series type.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-17 "Direct link to Returns")

`TSeriesType`

Type of the series.

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-7 "Direct link to Example")

    const lineSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries);console.log(lineSeries.seriesType()); // "Line"const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();console.log(candlestickSeries.seriesType()); // "Candlestick"

* * *

### lastValueData()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#lastvaluedata "Direct link to lastValueData()")

> **lastValueData**(`globalLast`): [`LastValueDataResult`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LastValueDataResult)

Return the last value data of the series.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-12 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **globalLast**: `boolean`

If false, get the last value in the current visible range. Otherwise, fetch the absolute last value

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-18 "Direct link to Returns")

[`LastValueDataResult`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LastValueDataResult)

The last value data of the series.

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#example-8 "Direct link to Example")

    const lineSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries);console.log(lineSeries.lastValueData(true)); // { noData: false, price: 24.11, color: '#000000' }const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();console.log(candlestickSeries.lastValueData(false)); // { noData: false, price: 145.72, color: '#000000' }

* * *

### attachPrimitive()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#attachprimitive "Direct link to attachPrimitive()")

> **attachPrimitive**(`primitive`): `void`

Attaches additional drawing primitive to the series

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-13 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **primitive**: [`ISeriesPrimitive`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/ISeriesPrimitive)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

any implementation of ISeriesPrimitive interface

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-19 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### detachPrimitive()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#detachprimitive "Direct link to detachPrimitive()")

> **detachPrimitive**(`primitive`): `void`

Detaches additional drawing primitive from the series

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-14 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **primitive**: [`ISeriesPrimitive`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/ISeriesPrimitive)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

implementation of ISeriesPrimitive interface attached before Does nothing if specified primitive was not attached

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-20 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### moveToPane()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#movetopane "Direct link to moveToPane()")

> **moveToPane**(`paneIndex`): `void`

Move the series to another pane.

If the pane with the specified index does not exist, the pane will be created.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-15 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **paneIndex**: `number`

The index of the pane. Should be a number between 0 and the total number of panes.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-21 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### seriesOrder()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#seriesorder "Direct link to seriesOrder()")

> **seriesOrder**(): `number`

Gets the zero-based index of this series within the list of all series on the current pane.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-22 "Direct link to Returns")

`number`

The current index of the series in the pane's series collection.

* * *

### setSeriesOrder()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setseriesorder "Direct link to setSeriesOrder()")

> **setSeriesOrder**(`order`): `void`

Sets the zero-based index of this series within the pane's series collection, thereby adjusting its rendering order.

Note:

*   The chart may automatically recalculate this index after operations such as removing other series or moving this series to a different pane.
*   If the provided index is less than 0, equal to, or greater than the number of series, it will be clamped to a valid range.
*   Price scales derive their formatters from the series with the lowest index; changing the order may affect the price scale's formatting

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **order**: `number`

The desired zero-based index to set for this series within the pane.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-23 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getPane()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#getpane "Direct link to getPane()")

> **getPane**(): [`IPaneApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Returns the pane to which the series is currently attached.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#returns-24 "Direct link to Returns")

[`IPaneApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IPaneApi)
<`HorzScaleItem`\>

Pane API object to control the pane

# Interface: SeriesAttachedParameter\<HorzScaleItem, TSeriesType\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Object containing references to the chart and series instances, and a requestUpdate method for triggering a refresh of the chart.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

• **TSeriesType** _extends_ [`SeriesType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/SeriesType)
 = keyof [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#chart "Direct link to chart")

> **chart**: [`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Chart instance.

* * *

### series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#series "Direct link to series")

> **series**: [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`TSeriesType`, `HorzScaleItem`, [`SeriesDataItemTypeMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDataItemTypeMap)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>\[`TSeriesType`\], [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
> \[`TSeriesType`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
> \[`TSeriesType`\]>

Series to which the Primitive is attached.

* * *

### requestUpdate()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#requestupdate "Direct link to requestUpdate()")

> **requestUpdate**: () => `void`

Request an update (redraw the chart)

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### horzScaleBehavior[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#horzscalebehavior "Direct link to horzScaleBehavior")

> **horzScaleBehavior**: [`IHorzScaleBehavior`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IHorzScaleBehavior)
> <`HorzScaleItem`\>

Horizontal Scale Behaviour for the chart.

*   [Type parameters](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#type-parameters)
    
*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#properties)
    *   [chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#chart)
        
    *   [series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#series)
        
    *   [requestUpdate()](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#requestupdate)
        
    *   [horzScaleBehavior](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesAttachedParameter#horzscalebehavior)

### applyOptions()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveWrapper#applyoptions "Direct link to applyOptions()?")

> `optional` **applyOptions**: (`options`) => `void`

Applies options to the primitive.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesPrimitiveWrapper#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **options**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`Options`\>

Options to apply. The options are deeply merged with the current options.

# Interface: MouseEventParams\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents a mouse event.

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### time?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#time "Direct link to time?")

> `optional` **time**: `HorzScaleItem`

Time of the data at the location of the mouse event.

The value will be `undefined` if the location of the event in the chart is outside the range of available data.

* * *

### logical?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#logical "Direct link to logical?")

> `optional` **logical**: [`Logical`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Logical)

Logical index

* * *

### point?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#point "Direct link to point?")

> `optional` **point**: [`Point`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/Point)

Location of the event in the chart.

The value will be `undefined` if the event is fired outside the chart, for example a mouse leave event.

* * *

### paneIndex?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/MouseEventParams#paneindex "Direct link to paneIndex?")

> `optional` **paneIndex**: `number`

The index of the Pane

* * *

> <`HorzScaleItem`\>>

Data of all series at the location of the event in the chart.

Keys of the map are [ISeriesApi](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
 instances. Values are prices. Values of the map are original data items

# Interface: TrackingModeOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### exitMode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#exitmode "Direct link to exitMode")

> **exitMode**: [`TrackingModeExitMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/TrackingModeExitMode)

Determine how to exit the tracking mode.

By default, mobile users will long press to deactivate the scroll and have the ability to check values and dates. Another press is required to activate the scroll, be able to move left/right, zoom, etc.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link TrackingModeExitMode.OnNextTap}

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#properties)
    *   [exitMode](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions#exitmode)

---

>  <[`LineStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
> \> | [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
>  <[`HistogramStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HistogramStyleOptions)
>  & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)

> \>>

Returns the current series.

> `optional` **applyOptions**: (`options`) => `void`

Applies options to the primitive.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesMarkersPluginApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

<`unknown`\>

Options to apply. The options are deeply merged with the current options.

`void`

# Interface: PriceChartLocalizationOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Extends LocalizationOptions for price-based charts. Includes settings specific to formatting price values on the horizontal scale.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
     <[`HorzScalePriceItem`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/HorzScalePriceItem)
    \>

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### timeFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#timeformatter "Direct link to timeFormatter?")

> `optional` **timeFormatter**: [`TimeFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TimeFormatterFn)
> <`number`\>

Override formatting of the time scale crosshair label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`timeFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#timeformatter)

* * *

### dateFormat[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#dateformat "Direct link to dateFormat")

> **dateFormat**: `string`

Date formatting string.

Can contain `yyyy`, `yy`, `MMMM`, `MMM`, `MM` and `dd` literals which will be replaced with corresponding date's value.

Ignored if [timeFormatter](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#timeformatter)
 has been specified.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'dd MMM \'yy'`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`dateFormat`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#dateformat)

* * *

### locale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#locale "Direct link to locale")

> **locale**: `string`

Current locale used to format dates. Uses the browser's language settings by default.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#see "Direct link to See")

[https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global\_Objects/Intl#Locale\_identification\_and\_negotiation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl#Locale_identification_and_negotiation)

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`navigator.language`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`locale`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#locale)

* * *

### priceFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#priceformatter "Direct link to priceFormatter?")

> `optional` **priceFormatter**: [`PriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceFormatterFn)

Override formatting of the price scale tick marks, labels and crosshair labels. Can be used for cases that can't be covered with built-in price formats.

#### See[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#see-1 "Direct link to See")

[PriceFormatCustom](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceFormatCustom)

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`priceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#priceformatter)

* * *

### tickmarksPriceFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#tickmarkspriceformatter "Direct link to tickmarksPriceFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickmarksPriceFormatter**: [`TickmarksPriceFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickmarksPriceFormatterFn)

Overrides the formatting of price scale tick marks. Use this to define formatting rules based on all provided price values.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`tickmarksPriceFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#tickmarkspriceformatter)

* * *

### percentageFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#percentageformatter "Direct link to percentageFormatter?")

> `optional` **percentageFormatter**: [`PercentageFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PercentageFormatterFn)

Overrides the formatting of percentage scale tick marks.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-5 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`percentageFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#percentageformatter)

* * *

### tickmarksPercentageFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#tickmarkspercentageformatter "Direct link to tickmarksPercentageFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickmarksPercentageFormatter**: [`TickmarksPercentageFormatterFn`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickmarksPercentageFormatterFn)

Override formatting of the percentage scale tick marks. Can be used if formatting should be adjusted based on all the values being formatted

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#inherited-from-6 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
 . [`tickmarksPercentageFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions#tickmarkspercentageformatter)

* * *

### precision[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#precision "Direct link to precision")

> **precision**: `number`

The number of decimal places to display for price values on the horizontal scale.

*   [Extends](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#extends)
    
*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#properties)
    *   [timeFormatter?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#timeformatter)

    *   [priceFormatter?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/PriceChartLocalizationOptions#priceformatter)

# Interface: SeriesStyleOptionsMap | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents the type of style options for each series type.

For example a bar series has style options represented by [BarStyleOptions](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions)
.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bar[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#bar "Direct link to Bar")

> **Bar**: [`BarStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BarStyleOptions)

The type of bar style options.

* * *

### Candlestick[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#candlestick "Direct link to Candlestick")

> **Candlestick**: [`CandlestickStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CandlestickStyleOptions)

The type of candlestick style options.

* * *

### Area[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#area "Direct link to Area")

> **Area**: [`AreaStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AreaStyleOptions)

The type of area style options.

* * *

### Baseline[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#baseline "Direct link to Baseline")

> **Baseline**: [`BaselineStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/BaselineStyleOptions)

The type of baseline style options.

* * *

### Line[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#line "Direct link to Line")

> **Line**: [`LineStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineStyleOptions)

The type of line style options.

* * *

### Histogram[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#histogram "Direct link to Histogram")

> **Histogram**: [`HistogramStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HistogramStyleOptions)

The type of histogram style options.

* * *

### Custom[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesStyleOptionsMap#custom "Direct link to Custom")

> **Custom**: [`CustomStyleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomStyleOptions)

The type of a custom series' style options.
