# Interface: WhitespaceData\<HorzScaleItem\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents a whitespace data item, which is a data point without a value.

Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData#example "Direct link to Example")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    const data = [    { time: '2018-12-03', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-04' }, // whitespace    { time: '2018-12-05' }, // whitespace    { time: '2018-12-06' }, // whitespace    { time: '2018-12-07' }, // whitespace    { time: '2018-12-08', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-13', value: 30.74 },];

Extended by[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData#extended-by "Direct link to Extended by")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`OhlcData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/OhlcData)
    
*   [`SingleValueData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SingleValueData)
    

Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **HorzScaleItem** = [`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Interface: TouchMouseEventData | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

The TouchMouseEventData interface represents events that occur due to the user interacting with a pointing device (such as a mouse). See [MouseEvent](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/MouseEvent)

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### clientX[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#clientx "Direct link to clientX")

> `readonly` **clientX**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The X coordinate of the mouse pointer in local (DOM content) coordinates.

* * *

### clientY[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#clienty "Direct link to clientY")

> `readonly` **clientY**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The Y coordinate of the mouse pointer in local (DOM content) coordinates.

* * *

### pageX[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#pagex "Direct link to pageX")

> `readonly` **pageX**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The X coordinate of the mouse pointer relative to the whole document.

* * *

### pageY[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#pagey "Direct link to pageY")

> `readonly` **pageY**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The Y coordinate of the mouse pointer relative to the whole document.

* * *

### screenX[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#screenx "Direct link to screenX")

> `readonly` **screenX**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The X coordinate of the mouse pointer in global (screen) coordinates.

* * *

### screenY[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#screeny "Direct link to screenY")

> `readonly` **screenY**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The Y coordinate of the mouse pointer in global (screen) coordinates.

* * *

### localX[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#localx "Direct link to localX")

> `readonly` **localX**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The X coordinate of the mouse pointer relative to the chart / price axis / time axis canvas element.

* * *

### localY[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#localy "Direct link to localY")

> `readonly` **localY**: [`Coordinate`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Coordinate)

The Y coordinate of the mouse pointer relative to the chart / price axis / time axis canvas element.

* * *

### ctrlKey[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#ctrlkey "Direct link to ctrlKey")

> `readonly` **ctrlKey**: `boolean`

Returns a boolean value that is true if the Ctrl key was active when the key event was generated.

* * *

### altKey[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#altkey "Direct link to altKey")

> `readonly` **altKey**: `boolean`

Returns a boolean value that is true if the Alt (Option or ⌥ on macOS) key was active when the key event was generated.

* * *

### shiftKey[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#shiftkey "Direct link to shiftKey")

> `readonly` **shiftKey**: `boolean`

Returns a boolean value that is true if the Shift key was active when the key event was generated.

* * *

### metaKey[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TouchMouseEventData#metakey "Direct link to metaKey")

> `readonly` **metaKey**: `boolean`

Returns a boolean value that is true if the Meta key (on Mac keyboards, the ⌘ Command key; on Windows keyboards, the Windows key (⊞)) was active when the key event was generated.

# Interface: SeriesMarkersOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Configuration options for the series markers plugin. These options affect all markers managed by the plugin.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autoScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#autoscale "Direct link to autoScale")

> **autoScale**: `boolean`

Specifies whether the auto-scaling calculation should expand to include the size of markers.

When `true`, the auto-scale feature will adjust the price scale's range to ensure series markers are fully visible and not cropped by the chart's edges.

When `false`, the scale will only fit the series data points, which may cause markers to be partially hidden.

Note: This option only has an effect when auto-scaling is enabled for the price scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### zOrder[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#zorder "Direct link to zOrder")

> **zOrder**: [`SeriesMarkerZOrder`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/SeriesMarkerZOrder)

Defines the stacking order of the markers relative to the series and other primitives.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`normal`

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#properties)
    *   [autoScale](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#autoscale)
        
    *   [zOrder](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesMarkersOptions#zorder)

---

Version: 5.1

On this page

Extended time scale options for time-based horizontal scale

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
    

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### rightOffset[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#rightoffset "Direct link to rightOffset")

> **rightOffset**: `number`

The margin space in bars from the right side of the chart.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`0`

 . [`rightOffset`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightoffset)

### rightOffsetPixels?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#rightoffsetpixels "Direct link to rightOffsetPixels?")

> `optional` **rightOffsetPixels**: `number`

The margin space in pixels from the right side of the chart. This option has priority over `rightOffset`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`rightOffsetPixels`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightoffsetpixels)

* * *

### barSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#barspacing "Direct link to barSpacing")

> **barSpacing**: `number`

The space between bars in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`6`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`barSpacing`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#barspacing)

* * *

> **minBarSpacing**: `number`

The minimum space between bars in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`0.5`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`minBarSpacing`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#minbarspacing)

* * *

### maxBarSpacing[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#maxbarspacing "Direct link to maxBarSpacing")

> **maxBarSpacing**: `number`

The maximum space between bars in pixels.

Has no effect if value is set to `0`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`0`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`maxBarSpacing`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#maxbarspacing)

* * *

> **fixLeftEdge**: `boolean`

Prevent scrolling to the left of the first bar.

`false`

> **fixRightEdge**: `boolean`

`false`

 . [`fixRightEdge`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#fixrightedge)

> **lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize**: `boolean`

Prevent changing the visible time range during chart resizing.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

 . [`lockVisibleTimeRangeOnResize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#lockvisibletimerangeonresize)

* * *

> **rightBarStaysOnScroll**: `boolean`

Prevent the hovered bar from moving when scrolling.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-8 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`rightBarStaysOnScroll`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#rightbarstaysonscroll)

* * *

### borderVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#bordervisible "Direct link to borderVisible")

> **borderVisible**: `boolean`

Show the time scale border.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-9 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`borderVisible`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#bordervisible)

* * *

### borderColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#bordercolor "Direct link to borderColor")

> **borderColor**: `string`

The time scale border color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#2B2B43'`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-10 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`borderColor`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#bordercolor)

* * *

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Show the time scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-11 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`visible`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#visible)

* * *

### timeVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#timevisible "Direct link to timeVisible")

> **timeVisible**: `boolean`

Show the time, not just the date, in the time scale and vertical crosshair label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`timeVisible`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#timevisible)

* * *

### secondsVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#secondsvisible "Direct link to secondsVisible")

> **secondsVisible**: `boolean`

Show seconds in the time scale and vertical crosshair label in `hh:mm:ss` format for intraday data.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-13 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`secondsVisible`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#secondsvisible)

* * *

### shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#shiftvisiblerangeonnewbar "Direct link to shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar")

> **shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar**: `boolean`

Shift the visible range to the right (into the future) by the number of new bars when new data is added.

Note that this only applies when the last bar is visible.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-14 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#shiftvisiblerangeonnewbar)

* * *

### allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#allowshiftvisiblerangeonwhitespacereplacement "Direct link to allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement")

> **allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement**: `boolean`

Allow the visible range to be shifted to the right when a new bar is added which is replacing an existing whitespace time point on the chart.

Note that this only applies when the last bar is visible & `shiftVisibleRangeOnNewBar` is enabled.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-15 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-15 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`allowShiftVisibleRangeOnWhitespaceReplacement`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#allowshiftvisiblerangeonwhitespacereplacement)

* * *

### ticksVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#ticksvisible "Direct link to ticksVisible")

> **ticksVisible**: `boolean`

Draw small vertical line on time axis labels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-16 "Direct link to Default Value")

`false`

 . [`ticksVisible`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#ticksvisible)

> `optional` **tickMarkMaxCharacterLength**: `number`

Maximum tick mark label length. Used to override the default 8 character maximum length.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-17 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-17 "Direct link to Inherited from")

 . [`tickMarkMaxCharacterLength`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#tickmarkmaxcharacterlength)

* * *

### uniformDistribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#uniformdistribution "Direct link to uniformDistribution")

> **uniformDistribution**: `boolean`

Changes horizontal scale marks generation. With this flag equal to `true`, marks of the same weight are either all drawn or none are drawn at all.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-18 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`uniformDistribution`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#uniformdistribution)

* * *

### minimumHeight[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#minimumheight "Direct link to minimumHeight")

> **minimumHeight**: `number`

Define a minimum height for the time scale. Note: This value will be exceeded if the time scale needs more space to display it's contents.

Setting a minimum height could be useful for ensuring that multiple charts positioned in a horizontal stack each have an identical time scale height, or for plugins which require a bit more space within the time scale pane.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-18 "Direct link to Default Value")

    0

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-19 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`minimumHeight`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#minimumheight)

* * *

### allowBoldLabels[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#allowboldlabels "Direct link to allowBoldLabels")

> **allowBoldLabels**: `boolean`

Allow major time scale labels to be rendered in a bolder font weight.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-19 "Direct link to Default Value")

    true

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-20 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`allowBoldLabels`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#allowboldlabels)

* * *

### ignoreWhitespaceIndices[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#ignorewhitespaceindices "Direct link to ignoreWhitespaceIndices")

> **ignoreWhitespaceIndices**: `boolean`

Ignore time scale points containing only whitespace (for all series) when drawing grid lines, tick marks, and snapping the crosshair to time scale points.

For the yield curve chart type it defaults to `true`.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-20 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-21 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`ignoreWhitespaceIndices`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#ignorewhitespaceindices)

* * *

### enableConflation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#enableconflation "Direct link to enableConflation")

> **enableConflation**: `boolean`

Enable data conflation for performance optimization when bar spacing is very small. When enabled, multiple data points are automatically combined into single points when they would be rendered in less than 0.5 pixels of screen space. This significantly improves rendering performance for large datasets when zoomed out.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-21 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-22 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`enableConflation`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#enableconflation)

* * *

### conflationThresholdFactor?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#conflationthresholdfactor "Direct link to conflationThresholdFactor?")

> `optional` **conflationThresholdFactor**: `number`

Smoothing factor for conflation thresholds. Controls how aggressively conflation is applied. This can be used to create smoother-looking charts, especially useful for sparklines and small charts.

*   1.0 = conflate only when display can't show detail (default, performance-focused)
*   2.0 = conflate at 2x the display threshold (moderate smoothing)
*   4.0 = conflate at 4x the display threshold (strong smoothing)
*   8.0+ = very aggressive smoothing for very small charts

Higher values result in fewer data points being displayed, creating smoother but less detailed charts. This is particularly useful for sparklines and small charts where smooth appearance is prioritized over showing every data point.

Note: Should be used with continuous series types (line, area, baseline) for best visual results. Candlestick and bar series may look less natural with high smoothing factors.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-22 "Direct link to Default Value")

    1.0

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-23 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`conflationThresholdFactor`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#conflationthresholdfactor)

* * *

### precomputeConflationOnInit[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#precomputeconflationoninit "Direct link to precomputeConflationOnInit")

> **precomputeConflationOnInit**: `boolean`

Precompute conflation chunks for common levels right after data load. When enabled, the system will precompute conflation data in the background, which improves performance when zooming out but increases initial load time and memory usage.

Performance impact:

*   Initial load: +100-500ms depending on dataset size
*   Memory usage: +20-50% of original dataset size
*   Zoom performance: Significant improvement (10-100x faster)

Recommended for: Large datasets (>10K points) on machines with sufficient memory

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-23 "Direct link to Default Value")

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-24 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`precomputeConflationOnInit`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#precomputeconflationoninit)

* * *

### precomputeConflationPriority[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#precomputeconflationpriority "Direct link to precomputeConflationPriority")

> **precomputeConflationPriority**: `"background"` | `"user-visible"` | `"user-blocking"`

Priority used for background precompute tasks when the Prioritized Task Scheduling API is available.

Options:

*   'background': Lowest priority, tasks run only when the browser is idle
*   'user-visible': Medium priority, tasks run when they might affect visible content
*   'user-blocking': Highest priority, tasks run immediately and may block user interaction

Recommendation: Use 'background' for most cases to avoid impacting user experience. Only use higher priorities if conflation is critical for your application's functionality.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-24 "Direct link to Default Value")

    'background'

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#inherited-from-25 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`HorzScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions)
 . [`precomputeConflationPriority`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HorzScaleOptions#precomputeconflationpriority)

* * *

### tickMarkFormatter?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#tickmarkformatter "Direct link to tickMarkFormatter?")

> `optional` **tickMarkFormatter**: [`TickMarkFormatter`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/TickMarkFormatter)

Tick marks formatter can be used to customize tick marks labels on the time axis.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeScaleOptions#default-value-25 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

# Interface: ISeriesUpDownMarkerPluginApi\<HorzScaleItem, TData\> | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesUpDownMarkerPluginApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

UpDownMarkersPrimitive Plugin for showing the direction of price changes on the chart. This plugin can only be used with Line and Area series types.

1.  Manual control:

*   Use the `setMarkers` method to manually add markers to the chart. This will replace any existing markers.
*   Use `clearMarkers` to remove all markers.

2.  Automatic updates:

Use `setData` and `update` from this primitive instead of the those on the series to let the primitive handle the creation of price change markers automatically.

*   Use `setData` to initialize or replace all data points.
*   Use `update` to modify individual data points. This will automatically create markers for price changes on existing data points.
*   The `updateVisibilityDuration` option controls how long markers remain visible.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesUpDownMarkerPluginApi#extends "Direct link to Extends")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    <`HorzScaleItem`\>

# Interface: TextWatermarkLineOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#color "Direct link to color")

> **color**: `string`

Watermark color.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)'`

* * *

### text[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#text "Direct link to text")

> **text**: `string`

Text of the watermark. Word wrapping is not supported.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### fontSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#fontsize "Direct link to fontSize")

> **fontSize**: `number`

Font size in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`48`

* * *

### lineHeight?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#lineheight "Direct link to lineHeight?")

> `optional` **lineHeight**: `number`

Line height in pixels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`1.2 * fontSize`

* * *

### fontFamily[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#fontfamily "Direct link to fontFamily")

> **fontFamily**: `string`

Font family.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Trebuchet MS', Roboto, Ubuntu, sans-serif`

* * *

### fontStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#fontstyle "Direct link to fontStyle")

> **fontStyle**: `string`

Font style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

# Interface: SeriesOptionsCommon | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Represents options common for all types of series

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### lastValueVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#lastvaluevisible "Direct link to lastValueVisible")

> **lastValueVisible**: `boolean`

Visibility of the label with the latest visible price on the price scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`, `false` for yield curve charts

* * *

### title[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#title "Direct link to title")

> **title**: `string`

You can name series when adding it to a chart. This name will be displayed on the label next to the last value label.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### priceScaleId?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricescaleid "Direct link to priceScaleId?")

> `optional` **priceScaleId**: `string`

Target price scale to bind new series to.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'right'` if right scale is visible and `'left'` otherwise

* * *

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Visibility of the series. If the series is hidden, everything including price lines, baseline, price labels and markers, will also be hidden. Please note that hiding a series is not equivalent to deleting it, since hiding does not affect the timeline at all, unlike deleting where the timeline can be changed (some points can be deleted).

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### priceLineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricelinevisible "Direct link to priceLineVisible")

> **priceLineVisible**: `boolean`

Show the price line. Price line is a horizontal line indicating the last price of the series.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-4 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`, `false` for yield curve charts

* * *

### priceLineSource[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricelinesource "Direct link to priceLineSource")

> **priceLineSource**: [`PriceLineSource`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/PriceLineSource)

The source to use for the value of the price line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-5 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link PriceLineSource.LastBar}

* * *

### priceLineWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricelinewidth "Direct link to priceLineWidth")

> **priceLineWidth**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Width of the price line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-6 "Direct link to Default Value")

`1`

* * *

### priceLineColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricelinecolor "Direct link to priceLineColor")

> **priceLineColor**: `string`

Color of the price line. By default, its color is set by the last bar color (or by line color on Line and Area charts).

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-7 "Direct link to Default Value")

`''`

* * *

### priceLineStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#pricelinestyle "Direct link to priceLineStyle")

> **priceLineStyle**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Price line style.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-8 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.Dashed}

* * *

### priceFormat[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#priceformat "Direct link to priceFormat")

> **priceFormat**: [`PriceFormat`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/PriceFormat)

Price format.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-9 "Direct link to Default Value")

`{ type: 'price', precision: 2, minMove: 0.01 }`

* * *

### baseLineVisible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#baselinevisible "Direct link to baseLineVisible")

> **baseLineVisible**: `boolean`

Visibility of base line. Suitable for percentage and `IndexedTo100` scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-10 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### baseLineColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#baselinecolor "Direct link to baseLineColor")

> **baseLineColor**: `string`

Color of the base line in `IndexedTo100` mode.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-11 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'#B2B5BE'`

* * *

### baseLineWidth[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#baselinewidth "Direct link to baseLineWidth")

> **baseLineWidth**: [`LineWidth`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/LineWidth)

Base line width. Suitable for percentage and `IndexedTo10` scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-12 "Direct link to Default Value")

`1`

* * *

### baseLineStyle[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#baselinestyle "Direct link to baseLineStyle")

> **baseLineStyle**: [`LineStyle`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/enumerations/LineStyle)

Base line style. Suitable for percentage and indexedTo100 scales.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-13 "Direct link to Default Value")

    {@link LineStyle.Solid}

* * *

### autoscaleInfoProvider?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#autoscaleinfoprovider "Direct link to autoscaleInfoProvider?")

> `optional` **autoscaleInfoProvider**: [`AutoscaleInfoProvider`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/AutoscaleInfoProvider)

Override the default [AutoscaleInfo](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/AutoscaleInfo)
 provider. By default, the chart scales data automatically based on visible data range. However, for some reasons one could require overriding this behavior.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-14 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined`

#### Examples[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#examples "Direct link to Examples")

    const firstSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, {    autoscaleInfoProvider: () => ({        priceRange: {            minValue: 0,            maxValue: 100,        },    }),});

    const firstSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, {    autoscaleInfoProvider: () => ({        priceRange: {            minValue: 0,            maxValue: 100,        },        margins: {            above: 10,            below: 10,        },    }),});

    const firstSeries = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, {    autoscaleInfoProvider: original => {        const res = original();        if (res !== null) {            res.priceRange.minValue -= 10;            res.priceRange.maxValue += 10;        }        return res;    },});

* * *

### conflationThresholdFactor?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#conflationthresholdfactor "Direct link to conflationThresholdFactor?")

> `optional` **conflationThresholdFactor**: `number`

Conflation smoothing factor for this series. Overrides the global time scale option. Controls how aggressively conflation is applied specifically to this series.

*   1.0 = conflate only when display can't show detail (default, performance-focused)
*   2.0 = conflate at 2x the display threshold (moderate smoothing)
*   4.0 = conflate at 4x the display threshold (strong smoothing)
*   8.0+ = very aggressive smoothing for very small charts

Higher values result in fewer data points being displayed for this series, creating smoother but less detailed charts. This allows different series on the same chart to have different conflation levels.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon#default-value-15 "Direct link to Default Value")

`undefined` (uses global time scale option)

# Interface: TimeChartOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Options for chart with time at the horizontal scale

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
     <[`Time`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/Time)
    \>

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: `number`

Width of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`width`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#width)

* * *

### height[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#height "Direct link to height")

> **height**: `number`

Height of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`height`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#height)

* * *

### autoSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TimeChartOptions#autosize "Direct link to autoSize")

> **autoSize**: `boolean`

Setting this flag to `true` will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes.

This feature requires [`ResizeObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver)
 class to be available in the global scope. Note that calling code is responsible for providing a polyfill if required. If the global scope does not have `ResizeObserver`, a warning will appear and the flag will be ignored.

Please pay attention that `autoSize` option and explicit sizes options `width` and `height` don't conflict with one another. If you specify `autoSize` flag, then `width` and `height` options will be ignored unless `ResizeObserver` has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag `autoSize` could also be set with and unset with `applyOptions` function.

    const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, {    autoSize: true,});

> **trackingMode**: [`TrackingModeOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions)

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

# Interface: SeriesOptionsMap | Lightweight Charts

On this page

Represents the type of options for each series type.

For example a bar series has options represented by [BarSeriesOptions](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/BarSeriesOptions)
.

# Interface: UpDownMarkersPluginOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Configuration options for the UpDownMarkers plugin.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### positiveColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#positivecolor "Direct link to positiveColor")

> **positiveColor**: `string`

The color used for markers indicating a positive price change. This color will be applied to markers shown above data points where the price has increased.

* * *

### negativeColor[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#negativecolor "Direct link to negativeColor")

> **negativeColor**: `string`

The color used for markers indicating a negative price change. This color will be applied to markers shown below data points where the price has decreased.

* * *

### updateVisibilityDuration[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#updatevisibilityduration "Direct link to updateVisibilityDuration")

> **updateVisibilityDuration**: `number`

The duration (in milliseconds) for which update markers remain visible on the chart. After this duration, the markers will automatically disappear. Set to 0 for markers to remain indefinitely until the next update.

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#properties)
    *   [positiveColor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#positivecolor)
        
    *   [negativeColor](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#negativecolor)
        
    *   [updateVisibilityDuration](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/UpDownMarkersPluginOptions#updatevisibilityduration)

---

# Interface: YieldCurveOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Options specific to yield curve charts.

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### baseResolution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#baseresolution "Direct link to baseResolution")

> **baseResolution**: `number`

The smallest time unit for the yield curve, typically representing one month. This value determines the granularity of the time scale.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

    1

* * *

### minimumTimeRange[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#minimumtimerange "Direct link to minimumTimeRange")

> **minimumTimeRange**: `number`

The minimum time range to be displayed on the chart, in units of baseResolution. This ensures that the chart always shows at least this much time range, even if there's less data.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

    120 (10 years)

* * *

### startTimeRange[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#starttimerange "Direct link to startTimeRange")

> **startTimeRange**: `number`

The starting time value for the chart, in units of baseResolution. This determines where the time scale begins.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

    0

* * *

### formatTime()?[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#formattime "Direct link to formatTime()?")

> `optional` **formatTime**: (`months`) => `string`

Optional custom formatter for time values on the horizontal axis. If not provided, a default formatter will be used.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **months**: `number`

The number of months (or baseResolution units) to format

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

*   [Properties](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#properties)
    *   [baseResolution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#baseresolution)
        
    *   [minimumTimeRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#minimumtimerange)
        
    *   [startTimeRange](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#starttimerange)
        
    *   [formatTime()?](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions#formattime)

---

Version: 5.1

On this page

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### visible[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#visible "Direct link to visible")

> **visible**: `boolean`

Display the watermark.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

* * *

### horzAlign[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#horzalign "Direct link to horzAlign")

> **horzAlign**: [`HorzAlign`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/HorzAlign)

Horizontal alignment inside the chart area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'center'`

* * *

### vertAlign[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#vertalign "Direct link to vertAlign")

> **vertAlign**: [`VertAlign`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/VertAlign)

Vertical alignment inside the chart area.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`'center'`

> **lines**: [`TextWatermarkLineOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkLineOptions)
> \[\]

Text to be displayed within the watermark. Each item in the array is treated as new line.

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TextWatermarkOptions#default-value-3 "Direct link to Default Value")

`[]`

# Interface: IYieldCurveChartApi | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

The main interface of a single yield curve chart.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#extends "Direct link to Extends")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   `Omit` <[`IChartApiBase`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IChartApiBase)
    <`number`\>, `"addSeries"`\>

Methods[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#methods "Direct link to Methods")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### remove()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#remove "Direct link to remove()")

> **remove**(): `void`

Removes the chart object including all DOM elements. This is an irreversible operation, you cannot do anything with the chart after removing it.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Omit.remove`

* * *

### resize()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#resize "Direct link to resize()")

> **resize**(`width`, `height`, `forceRepaint`?): `void`

Sets fixed size of the chart. By default chart takes up 100% of its container.

If chart has the `autoSize` option enabled, and the ResizeObserver is available then the width and height values will be ignored.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

• **width**: `number`

Target width of the chart.

• **height**: `number`

Target height of the chart.

• **forceRepaint?**: `boolean`

True to initiate resize immediately. One could need this to get screenshot immediately after resize.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Omit.resize`

* * *

### addCustomSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#addcustomseries "Direct link to addCustomSeries()")

> **addCustomSeries**<`TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>(`customPaneView`, `customOptions`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`"Custom"`, `number`, [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
> <`number`\> | `TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

Creates a custom series with specified parameters.

A custom series is a generic series which can be extended with a custom renderer to implement chart types which the library doesn't support by default.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **TData** _extends_ [`CustomData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/CustomData)
<`number`\>

• **TOptions** _extends_ [`CustomSeriesOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/CustomSeriesOptions)

• **TPartialOptions** _extends_ [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\> = [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **customPaneView**: [`ICustomSeriesPaneView`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ICustomSeriesPaneView)
<`number`, `TData`, `TOptions`\>

A custom series pane view which implements the custom renderer.

• **customOptions?**: [`DeepPartial`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/DeepPartial)
<`TOptions` & [`SeriesOptionsCommon`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsCommon)
\>

Customization parameters of the series being created.

    const series = chart.addCustomSeries(myCustomPaneView);

• **paneIndex?**: `number`

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
<`"Custom"`, `number`, [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
<`number`\> | `TData`, `TOptions`, `TPartialOptions`\>

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Omit.addCustomSeries`

* * *

### removeSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#removeseries "Direct link to removeSeries()")

> **removeSeries**(`seriesApi`): `void`

### subscribeClick()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#subscribeclick "Direct link to subscribeClick()")

> **subscribeClick**(`handler`): `void`

Subscribe to the chart click event.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **handler**: [`MouseEventHandler`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/MouseEventHandler)
<`number`\>

Handler to be called on mouse click.

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

`Omit.subscribeClick`

#### Example[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

    function myClickHandler(param) {    if (!param.point) {        return;    }    console.log(`Click at ${param.point.x}, ${param.point.y}. The time is ${param.time}.`);}chart.subscribeClick(myClickHandler);

* * *

### takeScreenshot()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#takescreenshot "Direct link to takeScreenshot()")

> **takeScreenshot**(`addTopLayer`?, `includeCrosshair`?): `HTMLCanvasElement`

Make a screenshot of the chart with all the elements excluding crosshair.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters-11 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **addTopLayer?**: `boolean`

if true, the top layer and primitives will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

• **includeCrosshair?**: `boolean`

works only if addTopLayer is enabled. If true, the crosshair will be included in the screenshot (default: false)

#### Returns[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#returns-14 "Direct link to Returns")

`HTMLCanvasElement`

A canvas with the chart drawn on. Any `Canvas` methods like `toDataURL()` or `toBlob()` can be used to serialize the result.

### setCrosshairPosition()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#setcrosshairposition "Direct link to setCrosshairPosition()")

> **setCrosshairPosition**(`price`, `horizontalPosition`, `seriesApi`): `void`

Set the crosshair position within the chart.

Usually the crosshair position is set automatically by the user's actions. However in some cases you may want to set it explicitly.

For example if you want to synchronise the crosshairs of two separate charts.

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters-15 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **price**: `number`

The price (vertical coordinate) of the new crosshair position.

• **horizontalPosition**: `number`

The horizontal coordinate (time by default) of the new crosshair position.

• **seriesApi**: [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)

### addSeries()[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#addseries "Direct link to addSeries()")

> **addSeries**<`T`\>(`definition`, `options`?, `paneIndex`?): [`ISeriesApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
> <`T`, `number`, [`WhitespaceData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/WhitespaceData)
> <`number`\> | [`LineData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LineData)
> <`number`\>, [`SeriesOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\], [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
> \[`T`\]>

Creates a series with specified parameters.

Note that the Yield Curve chart only supports the Area and Line series types.

#### Type parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

• **T** _extends_ [`YieldCurveSeriesType`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/type-aliases/YieldCurveSeriesType)

#### Parameters[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/IYieldCurveChartApi#parameters-17 "Direct link to Parameters")

• **definition**: [`SeriesDefinition`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesDefinition)
<`T`\>

A series definition for either AreaSeries or LineSeries.

• **options?**: [`SeriesPartialOptionsMap`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/SeriesPartialOptionsMap)
\[`T`\]

Customization parameters of the series being created.

• **paneIndex?**: `number`

An index of the pane where the series should be created.

    const series = chart.addSeries(LineSeries, { lineWidth: 2 });

# Interface: YieldCurveChartOptions | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

Version: 5.1

On this page

Extended chart options that include yield curve specific options. This interface combines the standard chart options with yield curve options.

Extends[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#extends "Direct link to Extends")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
    <`number`\>

Properties[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### width[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#width "Direct link to width")

> **width**: `number`

Width of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#default-value "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`width`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#width)

* * *

### height[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#height "Direct link to height")

> **height**: `number`

Height of the chart in pixels

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#default-value-1 "Direct link to Default Value")

If `0` (default) or none value provided, then a size of the widget will be calculated based its container's size.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`height`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#height)

* * *

### autoSize[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#autosize "Direct link to autoSize")

> **autoSize**: `boolean`

Setting this flag to `true` will make the chart watch the chart container's size and automatically resize the chart to fit its container whenever the size changes.

This feature requires [`ResizeObserver`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ResizeObserver)
 class to be available in the global scope. Note that calling code is responsible for providing a polyfill if required. If the global scope does not have `ResizeObserver`, a warning will appear and the flag will be ignored.

Please pay attention that `autoSize` option and explicit sizes options `width` and `height` don't conflict with one another. If you specify `autoSize` flag, then `width` and `height` options will be ignored unless `ResizeObserver` has failed. If it fails then the values will be used as fallback.

The flag `autoSize` could also be set with and unset with `applyOptions` function.

    const chart = LightweightCharts.createChart(document.body, {    autoSize: true,});

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`autoSize`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#autosize)

* * *
