* * *

### grid[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#grid "Direct link to grid")

> **grid**: [`GridOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/GridOptions)

A grid is represented in the chart background as a vertical and horizontal lines drawn at the levels of visible marks of price and the time scales.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-9 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`grid`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#grid)

* * *

### handleScroll[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#handlescroll "Direct link to handleScroll")

> **handleScroll**: `boolean` | [`HandleScrollOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScrollOptions)

Scroll options, or a boolean flag that enables/disables scrolling

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-10 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`handleScroll`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#handlescroll)

* * *

### handleScale[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#handlescale "Direct link to handleScale")

> **handleScale**: `boolean` | [`HandleScaleOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/HandleScaleOptions)

Scale options, or a boolean flag that enables/disables scaling

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-11 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`handleScale`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#handlescale)

* * *

### kineticScroll[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#kineticscroll "Direct link to kineticScroll")

> **kineticScroll**: [`KineticScrollOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/KineticScrollOptions)

Kinetic scroll options

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`kineticScroll`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#kineticscroll)

* * *

### trackingMode[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#trackingmode "Direct link to trackingMode")

> **trackingMode**: [`TrackingModeOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/TrackingModeOptions)

Represent options for the tracking mode's behavior.

Mobile users will not have the ability to see the values/dates like they do on desktop. To see it, they should enter the tracking mode. The tracking mode will deactivate the scrolling and make it possible to check values and dates.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`trackingMode`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#trackingmode)

* * *

### addDefaultPane[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#adddefaultpane "Direct link to addDefaultPane")

> **addDefaultPane**: `boolean`

Whether to add a default pane to the chart Disable this option when you want to create a chart with no panes and add them manually

#### Default Value[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#default-value-2 "Direct link to Default Value")

`true`

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`addDefaultPane`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#adddefaultpane)

* * *

### localization[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#localization "Direct link to localization")

> **localization**: [`LocalizationOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/LocalizationOptions)
> <`number`\>

Localization options.

#### Inherited from[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#inherited-from-15 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[`ChartOptionsImpl`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl)
 . [`localization`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/ChartOptionsImpl#localization)

* * *

### yieldCurve[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveChartOptions#yieldcurve "Direct link to yieldCurve")

> **yieldCurve**: [`YieldCurveOptions`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/api/interfaces/YieldCurveOptions)

Yield curve specific options. This object contains all the settings related to how the yield curve is displayed and behaves.

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is unreleased documentation for Lightweight Charts **Next** version.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: Next

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library that is not designed to work on the server side, for example, with Node.js.

The library code targets the [_ES2020_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/11.0/)
. Therefore, the browsers you work with should support this language revision. Consider the following [table](https://compat-table.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
 to ensure the browser compatibility.

To support previous revisions, you can set up a transpilation process for the `lightweight-charts` package in your build system using tools such as Babel. If you encounter any issues, open a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/issues)
 with detailed information, and we will investigate potential solutions.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#installation "Direct link to Installation")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To set up the library, install the [`lightweight-charts`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/lightweight-charts)
 npm package:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

The package includes TypeScript declarations, enabling seamless integration within TypeScript projects.

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Lightweight Charts™ license **requires** specifying TradingView as the product creator. You should add the following attributes to a public page of your website or mobile application:

*   Attribution notice from the [`NOTICE`](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
     file
*   The [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
     link

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a first step, import the library to your file:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

To create a chart, use the [`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/functions/createChart)
 function. You can call the function multiple times to create as many charts as needed:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...const firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

As a result, `createChart` returns an [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object that allows you to interact with the created chart.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the chart is created, you can display data on it.

The basic primitive to display data is a [series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. The library supports the following series types:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series, use the [`addSeries`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/IChartApi#addseries)
 method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. As a parameter, specify a [series type](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/series-types)
 you would like to create:

    import { AreaSeries, BarSeries, BaselineSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);const barSeries = chart.addSeries(BarSeries);const baselineSeries = chart.addSeries(BaselineSeries);// ...

Note that a series **cannot be transferred** from one type to another one, since different series types require different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the series is created, you can populate it with data. Note that the API calls remain the same regardless of the series type, although the data format may vary.

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data to a series, you should call the [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: 'black', background: { type: 'solid', color: 'white' } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries, {    lineColor: '#2962FF', topColor: '#2962FF',    bottomColor: 'rgba(41, 98, 255, 0.28)',});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries, {    upColor: '#26a69a', downColor: '#ef5350', borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: '#26a69a', wickDownColor: '#ef5350',});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

You can also use `setData` to replace all data items.

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

If your data is updated, for example in real-time, you may also need to refresh the chart accordingly. To do this, call the [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
 method that allows you to update the last data item or add a new one.

    import { AreaSeries, CandlestickSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);areaSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries);candlestickSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// ...// Update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// Creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

We do not recommend calling [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 to update the chart, as this method replaces all series data and can significantly affect the performance.

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/next#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

---

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for Lightweight Charts **5.0**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: 5.0

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library that is not designed to work on the server side, for example, with Node.js.

The library code targets the [_ES2020_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/11.0/)
. Therefore, the browsers you work with should support this language revision. Consider the following [table](https://compat-table.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
 to ensure the browser compatibility.

To support previous revisions, you can set up a transpilation process for the `lightweight-charts` package in your build system using tools such as Babel. If you encounter any issues, open a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/issues)
 with detailed information, and we will investigate potential solutions.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To set up the library, install the [`lightweight-charts`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/lightweight-charts)
 npm package:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

The package includes TypeScript declarations, enabling seamless integration within TypeScript projects.

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Lightweight Charts™ license **requires** specifying TradingView as the product creator. You should add the following attributes to a public page of your website or mobile application:

*   Attribution notice from the [`NOTICE`](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
     file
*   The [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
     link

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As a first step, import the library to your file:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

To create a chart, use the [`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/functions/createChart)
 function. You can call the function multiple times to create as many charts as needed:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...const firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

As a result, `createChart` returns an [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object that allows you to interact with the created chart.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the chart is created, you can display data on it.

The basic primitive to display data is a [series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. The library supports the following series types:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series, use the [`addSeries`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/IChartApi#addseries)
 method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. As a parameter, specify a [series type](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/series-types)
 you would like to create:

    import { AreaSeries, BarSeries, BaselineSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);const barSeries = chart.addSeries(BarSeries);const baselineSeries = chart.addSeries(BaselineSeries);// ...

Note that a series **cannot be transferred** from one type to another one, since different series types require different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the series is created, you can populate it with data. Note that the API calls remain the same regardless of the series type, although the data format may vary.

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data to a series, you should call the [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: 'black', background: { type: 'solid', color: 'white' } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries, {    lineColor: '#2962FF', topColor: '#2962FF',    bottomColor: 'rgba(41, 98, 255, 0.28)',});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries, {    upColor: '#26a69a', downColor: '#ef5350', borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: '#26a69a', wickDownColor: '#ef5350',});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

You can also use `setData` to replace all data items.

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

If your data is updated, for example in real-time, you may also need to refresh the chart accordingly. To do this, call the [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
 method that allows you to update the last data item or add a new one.

    import { AreaSeries, CandlestickSeries, createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addSeries(AreaSeries);areaSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addSeries(CandlestickSeries);candlestickSeries.setData([    // Other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// ...// Update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// Creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

We do not recommend calling [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 to update the chart, as this method replaces all series data and can significantly affect the performance.

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/5.0#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for Lightweight Charts **4.2**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: 4.2

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First of all, Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library. This means that it does not and cannot work on the server-side (i.e. NodeJS), at least out of the box.

The code of `lightweight-charts` package targets the [_es2016_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/7.0/)
. Thus, all the browsers you will have to work with should support this language revision (see [this compatibility table](https://kangax.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
). If you need to support the previous revisions, you could try to setup a transpilation of the package to the target you need to support in your build system (e.g. by using Babel). If you'll have any issues with that, please raise an issue on github with the details and we'll investigate possible ways to solve it.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The first thing you need to do to use `lightweight-charts` is to install it from [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/)
:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

_Note that the package is shipped with TypeScript declarations, so you can easily use it within TypeScript code._

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | CommonJS ⚠️ | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.production.cjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.development.cjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

⚠️ **Deprecation note:** CommonJS support will be removed from the library at the start of 2024.

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tip

The Lightweight Charts™ license requires specifying TradingView as the product creator.

You shall add the "attribution notice" from the [NOTICE](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
 file and a link to [https://www.tradingview.com/](https://www.tradingview.com/)
 to the page of your website or mobile application that is available to your users.

As thanks for creating Lightweight Charts™, we'd be grateful if you add the attribution notice in a prominent place.

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the library has been installed in your repo you're ready to create your first chart.

First of all, in a file where you would like to create a chart you need to import the library:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

[`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/functions/createChart)
 is the entry-point for creating charts. You can use it to create as many charts as you need:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...// somewhere in your codeconst firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

The result of this function is a [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object, which you need to use to work with a chart instance.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart is created it is ready to display data.

The basic primitive to display a data is [a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. There are different types of series:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series with desired type you need to use appropriate method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. All of them have the same naming `add<type>Series`, where `<type>` is a type of a series you'd like to create:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();const barSeries = chart.addBarSeries();const baselineSeries = chart.addBaselineSeries();// ... and so on

Please look at [this page](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/series-types)
 for more information about different series types.

Note that **a series cannot be transferred from one type to another one** since different series types have different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart and series are created it's time to set data to the series.

Note that regardless of the series type, the API calls are the same (the type of the data might be different though).

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data (or to replace all data items) to a series you need to use [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: CHART_TEXT_COLOR, background: { type: 'solid', color: CHART_BACKGROUND_COLOR } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries({    lineColor: LINE_LINE_COLOR, topColor: AREA_TOP_COLOR,    bottomColor: AREA_BOTTOM_COLOR,});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries({    upColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, downColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR, borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, wickDownColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR,});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

In a case when your data is updated (e.g. real-time updates) you might want to update the chart as well.

But using [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 very often might affect the performance and we do not recommend to do this. Also it replaces all series data with the new one, and probably this is not what you're looking for.

Thus, to update the data you can use a method [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
. It allows you to update the last data item or add a new one much faster without affecting the performance:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();areaSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();candlestickSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// sometime later// update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.2#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

---

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for Lightweight Charts **4.1**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: 4.1

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First of all, Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library. This means that it does not and cannot work on the server-side (i.e. NodeJS), at least out of the box.

The code of `lightweight-charts` package targets the [_es2016_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/7.0/)
. Thus, all the browsers you will have to work with should support this language revision (see [this compatibility table](https://kangax.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
). If you need to support the previous revisions, you could try to setup a transpilation of the package to the target you need to support in your build system (e.g. by using Babel). If you'll have any issues with that, please raise an issue on github with the details and we'll investigate possible ways to solve it.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The first thing you need to do to use `lightweight-charts` is to install it from [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/)
:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

_Note that the package is shipped with TypeScript declarations, so you can easily use it within TypeScript code._

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | CommonJS ⚠️ | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.production.cjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.development.cjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

⚠️ **Deprecation note:** CommonJS support will be removed from the library at the start of 2024.

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tip

The Lightweight Charts™ license requires specifying TradingView as the product creator.

You shall add the "attribution notice" from the [NOTICE](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
 file and a link to [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
 to the page of your website or mobile application that is available to your users.

As thanks for creating Lightweight Charts™, we'd be grateful if you add the attribution notice in a prominent place.

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the library has been installed in your repo you're ready to create your first chart.

First of all, in a file where you would like to create a chart you need to import the library:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

[`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/functions/createChart)
 is the entry-point for creating charts. You can use it to create as many charts as you need:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...// somewhere in your codeconst firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

The result of this function is a [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object, which you need to use to work with a chart instance.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart is created it is ready to display data.

The basic primitive to display a data is [a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. There are different types of series:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series with desired type you need to use appropriate method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. All of them have the same naming `add<type>Series`, where `<type>` is a type of a series you'd like to create:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();const barSeries = chart.addBarSeries();const baselineSeries = chart.addBaselineSeries();// ... and so on

Please look at [this page](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/series-types)
 for more information about different series types.

Note that **a series cannot be transferred from one type to another one** since different series types have different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart and series are created it's time to set data to the series.

Note that regardless of the series type, the API calls are the same (the type of the data might be different though).

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data (or to replace all data items) to a series you need to use [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: CHART_TEXT_COLOR, background: { type: 'solid', color: CHART_BACKGROUND_COLOR } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries({    lineColor: LINE_LINE_COLOR, topColor: AREA_TOP_COLOR,    bottomColor: AREA_BOTTOM_COLOR,});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries({    upColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, downColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR, borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, wickDownColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR,});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

In a case when your data is updated (e.g. real-time updates) you might want to update the chart as well.

But using [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 very often might affect the performance and we do not recommend to do this. Also it replaces all series data with the new one, and probably this is not what you're looking for.

Thus, to update the data you can use a method [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
. It allows you to update the last data item or add a new one much faster without affecting the performance:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();areaSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();candlestickSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// sometime later// update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.1#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

---

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for Lightweight Charts **4.0**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: 4.0

On this page

Requirements[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#requirements "Direct link to Requirements")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

First of all, Lightweight Charts™ is _a client-side_ library. This means that it does not and cannot work on the server-side (i.e. NodeJS), at least out of the box.

The code of `lightweight-charts` package is targeted to [_es2016_ language specification](https://262.ecma-international.org/7.0/)
. Thus, all the browsers you will have to work with should support this language revision (see [this compatibility table](https://kangax.github.io/compat-table/es2016plus/)
). If you need to support the previous revisions, you could try to setup a transpilation of the package to the target you need to support in your build system (e.g. by using Babel). If you'll have any issues with that, please raise an issue on github with the details and we'll investigate possible ways to solve it.

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The first thing you need to do to use `lightweight-charts` is to install it from [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/)
:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

_Note that the package is shipped with TypeScript declarations, so you can easily use it within TypeScript code._

### Build variants[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#build-variants "Direct link to Build variants")

The library ships with the following build variants:

| Dependencies included | Mode | ES module | CommonJS ⚠️ | IIFE (`window.LightweightCharts`) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| No  | PROD | `lightweight-charts.production.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.production.cjs` | N/A |
| No  | DEV | `lightweight-charts.development.mjs` | `lightweight-charts.development.cjs` | N/A |
| Yes (standalone) | PROD | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.production.js` |
| Yes (standalone) | DEV | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.mjs` | \-  | `lightweight-charts.standalone.development.js` |

⚠️ **Deprecation note:** CommonJS support will be removed from the library at the start of 2024.

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tip

The Lightweight Charts™ license requires specifying TradingView as the product creator.

You shall add the "attribution notice" from the [NOTICE](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/master/NOTICE)
 file and a link to [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
 to the page of your website or mobile application that is available to your users.

As thanks for creating Lightweight Charts™, we'd be grateful if you add the attribution notice in a prominent place.

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the library has been installed in your repo you're ready to create your first chart.

First of all, in a file where you would like to create a chart you need to import the library:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

[`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/functions/createChart)
 is the entry-point for creating charts. You can use it to create as many charts as you need:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...// somewhere in your codeconst firstChart = createChart(document.getElementById('firstContainer'));const secondChart = createChart(document.getElementById('secondContainer'));

The result of this function is a [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object, which you need to use to work with a chart instance.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart is created it is ready to display data.

The basic primitive to display a data is [a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. There are different types of series:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series with desired type you need to use appropriate method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. All of them have the same naming `add<type>Series`, where `<type>` is a type of a series you'd like to create:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();const barSeries = chart.addBarSeries();const baselineSeries = chart.addBaselineSeries();// ... and so on

Please look at [this page](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/series-types)
 for more information about different series types.

Note that **a series cannot be transferred from one type to another one** since different series types have different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart and series are created it's time to set data to the series.

Note that regardless of the series type, the API calls are the same (the type of the data might be different though).

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data (or to replace all data items) to a series you need to use [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chartOptions = { layout: { textColor: CHART_TEXT_COLOR, background: { type: 'solid', color: CHART_BACKGROUND_COLOR } } };const chart = createChart(document.getElementById('container'), chartOptions);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries({    lineColor: LINE_LINE_COLOR, topColor: AREA_TOP_COLOR,    bottomColor: AREA_BOTTOM_COLOR,});areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries({    upColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, downColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR, borderVisible: false,    wickUpColor: BAR_UP_COLOR, wickDownColor: BAR_DOWN_COLOR,});candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

In a case when your data is updated (e.g. real-time updates) you might want to update the chart as well.

But using [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 very often might affect the performance and we do not recommend to do this. Also it replaces all series data with the new one, and probably this is not what you're looking for.

Thus, to update the data you can use a method [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
. It allows you to update the last data item or add a new one much faster without affecting the performance:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();areaSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();candlestickSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// sometime later// update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

*   [Requirements](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#requirements)
    
*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#installation)
    *   [Build variants](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#build-variants)
        
*   [License and attribution](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#license-and-attribution)
    
*   [Creating a chart](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#creating-a-chart)
    
*   [Creating a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#creating-a-series)
    
*   [Setting and updating a data](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#setting-and-updating-a-data)
    *   [Setting the data to a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#setting-the-data-to-a-series)
        
    *   [Updating the data in a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/4.0#updating-the-data-in-a-series)

---

# Getting started | Lightweight Charts

[Skip to main content](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for Lightweight Charts **3.8**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs)
** (5.1).

Version: 3.8

On this page

Installation[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#installation "Direct link to Installation")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The first thing you need to do to use `lightweight-charts` is to install it from [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/)
:

    npm install --save lightweight-charts

_Note that the package is shipped with TypeScript declarations, so you can easily use it within TypeScript code._

License and attribution[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#license-and-attribution "Direct link to License and attribution")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

tip

The Lightweight Charts™ license requires specifying TradingView as the product creator.

You shall add the "attribution notice" from the [NOTICE](https://github.com/tradingview/lightweight-charts/blob/013424ecbf672db80646fe51ee48b187e9b71cec/NOTICE)
 file and a link to [https://www.tradingview.com](https://www.tradingview.com/)
 to the page of your website or mobile application that is available to your users.

As thanks for creating Lightweight Charts™, we'd be grateful if you add the attribution notice in a prominent place.

Creating a chart[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#creating-a-chart "Direct link to Creating a chart")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the library has been installed in your repo you're ready to create your first chart.

First of all, in a file where you would like to create a chart you need to import the library:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';

[`createChart`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/functions/createChart)
 is the entry-point for creating charts. You can use it to create as many charts as you need:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';// ...// somewhere in your codeconst firstChart = createChart(firstContainer);const secondChart = createChart(secondContainer);

The result of this function is a [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
 object, which you need to use to work with a chart instance.

Creating a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#creating-a-series "Direct link to Creating a series")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart is created it is ready to display data.

The basic primitive to display a data is [a series](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi)
. There are different types of series:

*   Area
*   Bar
*   Baseline
*   Candlestick
*   Histogram
*   Line

To create a series with desired type you need to use appropriate method from [`IChartApi`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/IChartApi)
. All of them have the same naming `add<type>Series`, where `<type>` is a type of a series you'd like to create:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();const barSeries = chart.addBarSeries();const baselineSeries = chart.addBaselineSeries();// ... and so on

Please look at [this page](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/series-types)
 for more information about different series types.

Note that **a series cannot be transferred from one type to another one** since different series types have different data and options types.

Setting and updating a data[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#setting-and-updating-a-data "Direct link to Setting and updating a data")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once your chart and series are created it's time to set data to the series.

Note that regardless of the series type, the API calls are the same (the type of the data might be different though).

### Setting the data to a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#setting-the-data-to-a-series "Direct link to Setting the data to a series")

To set the data (or to replace all data items) to a series you need to use [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 method:

    const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();areaSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', value: 32.51 },    { time: '2018-12-23', value: 31.11 },    { time: '2018-12-24', value: 27.02 },    { time: '2018-12-25', value: 27.32 },    { time: '2018-12-26', value: 25.17 },    { time: '2018-12-27', value: 28.89 },    { time: '2018-12-28', value: 25.46 },    { time: '2018-12-29', value: 23.92 },    { time: '2018-12-30', value: 22.68 },    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();candlestickSeries.setData([    { time: '2018-12-22', open: 75.16, high: 82.84, low: 36.16, close: 45.72 },    { time: '2018-12-23', open: 45.12, high: 53.90, low: 45.12, close: 48.09 },    { time: '2018-12-24', open: 60.71, high: 60.71, low: 53.39, close: 59.29 },    { time: '2018-12-25', open: 68.26, high: 68.26, low: 59.04, close: 60.50 },    { time: '2018-12-26', open: 67.71, high: 105.85, low: 66.67, close: 91.04 },    { time: '2018-12-27', open: 91.04, high: 121.40, low: 82.70, close: 111.40 },    { time: '2018-12-28', open: 111.51, high: 142.83, low: 103.34, close: 131.25 },    { time: '2018-12-29', open: 131.33, high: 151.17, low: 77.68, close: 96.43 },    { time: '2018-12-30', open: 106.33, high: 110.20, low: 90.39, close: 98.10 },    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);chart.timeScale().fitContent();

### Updating the data in a series[​](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#updating-the-data-in-a-series "Direct link to Updating the data in a series")

In a case when your data is updated (e.g. real-time updates) you might want to update the chart as well.

But using [`ISeriesApi.setData`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#setdata)
 very often might affect the performance and we do not recommend to do this. Also it replaces all series data with the new one, and probably this is not what you're looking for.

Thus, to update the data you can use a method [`ISeriesApi.update`](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8/api/interfaces/ISeriesApi#update)
. It allows you to update the last data item or add a new one much faster without affecting the performance:

    import { createChart } from 'lightweight-charts';const chart = createChart(container);const areaSeries = chart.addAreaSeries();areaSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', value: 22.67 },]);const candlestickSeries = chart.addCandlestickSeries();candlestickSeries.setData([    // ... other data items    { time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 111.26 },]);// sometime later// update the most recent barareaSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', value: 25 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2018-12-31', open: 109.87, high: 114.69, low: 85.66, close: 112 });// creating the new barareaSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', value: 20 });candlestickSeries.update({ time: '2019-01-01', open: 112, high: 112, low: 100, close: 101 });

*   [Installation](https://tradingview.github.io/lightweight-charts/docs/3.8#installation)
